issue_title: Extract notification timestamp and variable name in callback decorator
issue_body:
To relieve the user from the need of using ctypes data structure operations for evaluating the value of a changed variable a `notification` decorator has been introduced.

This decorator takes a datatype as an attribute to specify the datatype of the return value. This decorator should be enhanced so that it returns the name of the variable that was changed as well as the timestamp of the change as a `datetime` object.

issue_title: Document exceptions raised by Kernel.run
issue_body:
It seems that curio.run() may raise a TaskError. This is currently not documented, the documentation for Kernel.run does not mention any exceptions, and the documentation for TaskError states that this is only raised by a taks's join() method.

It would be nice to document the current behavior.

It would be even nicer (in my opinion) to also just re-raise the original exception. I don't see much point in wrapping it in a TaskError. In particular, when unit-testing a program that uses curio curoutines, this makes working with the tests rather awkward.

issue_title: Connection's recv buffer should be emptied before sending new command
issue_body:
In some case, if a command was interrupted by outer exceptions(such as timeout),  the connection will be released to the pool containing unconsumed data. The unconsumed data will be read by the next command and thus lead to mistake.

The following code can reproduce this situation (it use async_timeout library from aio-libs):
```
# coding:utf-8

from aredis import StrictRedisCluster
import asyncio
import random
import async_timeout
import time

redis_cluster_client = StrictRedisCluster(
    startup_nodes=[{"host": "127.0.0.1", "port": 7001}],
    max_connections=40, max_connections_per_node=True)


async def worker():
    while(1):
        try:
            with async_timeout.timeout(0.05):
                for _ in range(2):
                    await redis_cluster_client.exists("test_key")
                    await asyncio.sleep(random.random() / 100)
                await redis_cluster_client.set("test_key", "1")
                await asyncio.sleep(random.random() / 100)
        except Exception as e:
            print(e)


def main():
    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
    asyncio.gather(*[worker() for _ in range(30)], loop=loop)
    loop.run_forever()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```
And you will get output like this after running for sometime:
```
'int' object has no attribute 'decode'
'int' object has no attribute 'decode'
'int' object has no attribute 'decode'
'int' object has no attribute 'decode'
```

In the code above, a very small timeout value (0.05 second) is set, if you can't reproduce the error, you can try to reduce it to a even smaller one.

The `exists` command will return 0 or 1, and the `set` command will get the `int` type value returned for `exists` command and lead to a error.

In my production code, a worker will execute a job with a timeout limit, if the job timeout, it may cause this error.

issue_title: Option to use TCP as protocol instead of UDP when querying dns
issue_body:
Hi all,

  I've been using this library for a while but I started getting DNS timeouts recently, because the size of the response in my system increased. I believe this is related to the issue asked here: 
[when-do-dns-queries-use-tcp-instead-of-udp](https://serverfault.com/questions/404840/when-do-dns-queries-use-tcp-instead-of-udp)

By default the query will use UDP which doesn't work for long responses (over 512 or 4096 bytes).

  Using the extra parameter `tcp=True` in the `resolver.query` method solved my issue, but with the current code there is no way to pass this option to it.

  Would be OK to add this option in the `srvlookup.lookup` method?

  I can do the code changes myself, I just want to know if this is compatible with the vision for this library and if there is any design guidelines that I whould be aware of. 

Thanks!

issue_title: Support passing in a `Session` object
issue_body:
Users may already have a `requests.Session` object and wish to reuse that rather than having `httsleep` create a blank one.  This would save users from re-entering standard headers, settings etc. that they've already configured elsewhere.

### Ideal usage afterwards:
```python
    httsleep(url, until=until, alarms=alarms, session=my_session)
```

### ~~~Workaround~~~ Not usable
```python
    poller = HttSleeper(url, until=until, alarms=alarms)
    poller.session = my_session
    poller.run()
```
Not usable because [here](https://github.com/kopf/httsleep/blob/master/httsleep/main.py#L148) the request is prepared without the context of the Session:
```python
response = self.session.send(self.request.prepare()
```

The workaround would be usable if this line was instead:
```python
response = self.session.send(self.session.prepare_request(request))
```

Traceback (most recent call last):

         if len(self.binVal) != 16:

                 "UUID must be 16 bytes, got '%s' (len=%d)" % (val,
@@ -175,7 +175,7 @@ class Peripheral:
             elif tval[0]=="h":
                 val = int(tval[1:], 16)

issue_title: open_files() on Linux may erroneously raise NoSuchProcess
issue_body:
When iterating over `/proc/{pid}/fdinfo/{fd}` "fd" may disappear on us. In this case psutil treats this condition as "process is gone" and raises NoSuchProcess.
Instead, it should skip the fd and go on.
This is related to #1014.

issue_title: __exit__ should not return anything unless it has handled exceptions
issue_body:
Hi,
Currently, __exit__ returns the result of the logout call. This is incorrect, as it results in suppression of  all exceptions generated while using this as a context manager.

Specific line of code: https://github.com/p4r4n0y1ng/pyfmg/blob/master/pyFMG/fortimgr.py#L182

Relevant documentation: https://docs.python.org/2/reference/datamodel.html#object.__exit__

issue_title: bug in Path.Arc._parameterize
issue_body:
Arc((725.307482225571-915.5548199281527j), (202.79421639137703+148.77294617167183j), 225.6910319606926, 1, 1, (-624.6375539637027+896.5483089399895j))

raises a ValueError
From http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG/implnote.html#ArcImplementationNotes
looks like p/n is the inner product of two unit vectors, and thus will always be between -1 and 1.

using 
(p/n + 1) % 2 - 1
in the two places you take an inverse cosine will fix this.

issue_title: AssertionError when using ProactorEventLoop
issue_body:
The ProactorEventLoop is the only event loop on Windows that supports subprocesses, but trying to use it with Discord.py results in it spitting out an error and soft locking. 

Error message:
```
Exception in callback _ProactorReadPipeTransport._loop_reading(<_OverlappedF...op_reading()]>)
handle: <Handle _ProactorReadPipeTransport._loop_reading(<_OverlappedF...op_reading()]>)>
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "c:\python36\Lib\asyncio\events.py", line 127, in _run
    self._callback(*self._args)
  File "c:\python36\Lib\asyncio\proactor_events.py", line 190, in _loop_reading
    data = fut.result()  # deliver data later in "finally" clause
asyncio.base_futures.InvalidStateError: Result is not ready.
Exception in callback _ProactorReadPipeTransport._loop_reading(<_OverlappedF...\x92\xfd\xe9'>)
handle: <Handle _ProactorReadPipeTransport._loop_reading(<_OverlappedF...\x92\xfd\xe9'>)>
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "c:\python36\Lib\asyncio\events.py", line 127, in _run
    self._callback(*self._args)
  File "c:\python36\Lib\asyncio\proactor_events.py", line 188, in _loop_reading
    self._closing)
AssertionError
```

Source code to repro is just the README.md example modified to use Proactor before the Discord Client is initialized.
```
import discord

import asyncio, sys

if sys.platform == 'win32':
    loop = asyncio.ProactorEventLoop()
    asyncio.set_event_loop(loop)

client = discord.Client()

@client.event
async def on_ready():
    print('Logged in as')
    print(client.user.name)
    print(client.user.id)
    print('------')

@client.event
async def on_message(message):
    if message.content.startswith('!test'):
        counter = 0
        tmp = await client.send_message(message.channel, 'Calculating messages...')
        async for log in client.logs_from(message.channel, limit=100):
            if log.author == message.author:
                counter += 1

        await client.edit_message(tmp, 'You have {} messages.'.format(counter))
    elif message.content.startswith('!sleep'):
        await asyncio.sleep(5)
        await client.send_message(message.channel, 'Done sleeping')

client.run('token')
```
I'm running Windows 7 with Python 3.6.

issue_title: Category title transformation is language specific (categorytree is broken as a result for non-english languages)
issue_body:
The english prefix category:" is removed in mediawiki.py:

~~~
536                 elif rec['type'] == 'subcat':
537                     tmp = rec['title']
538                     if tmp.startswith('Category:'):                                                                                                                                         
539                         tmp = tmp[9:]
540                     subcats.append(tmp)
~~~

This does not work for non-english languages. Categorytree is broken for non-english languages, because each category is prefixed with "Category:".

~~~
606                     try:
607                         categories[cat] = self.page('Category:{0}'.format(cat))
608                         parent_cats = categories[cat].categories                                                                                                                            
609                         links[cat] = self.categorymembers(cat, results=None,
610                                                           subcategories=True)
611                         break
~~~

In e.g. german, this leads to the generation of: ``"Category:Kategorie:Kategorietitel"``. I see three ways (this is why I did not want to create a PR):

 - do not strip/prepend "Category:" and let the user handle it in their language
 - offer defaults and a setting for some languages (five with most articles or something like that?)
 -  query some wikipedia api to get the category prefix in the respective language.

I like it simple, so I would stick with the first solution :)

issue_title: Improve cronjob safety by moving shell script into Python code
issue_body:
Shouldn't the cronjob shell script go into the Python code base?

    python acme_tiny.py --renew-cert ...

I understand that the code base should stay small, but the renewal is a vital part of the system. Moving that out into a user-managed shell script means that any code audit will be incomplete. So it should be maintained in the Python code base which is easily updated, rather than in the README where some old version may have been copied into the system and never be updated again. Also, proper error handling is much easier in Python than in a shell script.

Moreover, issue #117 shows that this shell script is not optimal and may need further improvements. Do we really want to maintain these safety improvements in the README?

issue_title: Better way to detect consumer bootstrap failure when calling poll?
issue_body:
I'm wondering how should the client behave if bootstrapping fails for a KafkaConsumer.

Since a failing bootstrap does not raise an exception, if you just blindly start calling poll after init, the poll call will hang forever trying to connect. 

So what I'm getting at is that at least my current method of doing this is a bit cumbersome and I was wondering if it could be made more convenient somehow.

a) Would it be ok for polling to raise an exception when _bootstrap_fails > 0 since otherwise it will never exit.
or
b) for polling to automatically call consumer._client._bootstrap() again 

Currently what I'm doing is that at initialization time I have a while loop that looks something like: 

(pseudocode)
consumer = KafkaConsumer(hosts)
while True:
    if consumer._client._bootstrap_fails > 0:
       consumer._client._bootstrap(collect_hosts(hosts))
   elif consumer._client._boostrap_fails == 0:
       break

Which is a bit tedious.

issue_title: `Latest` points to 1.3.2 not 1.3.3
High CPU load when using Group Coordination
issue_body:
Using kafka.KafkaConsumer in multiple processes with the same group ID I'm noticing high CPU load when idle.  I'm running the kafka-python 0.9 branch (commit 31c3d59ee3507a65533a3db58dd0fa59d9925b11) against Kafka 0.9.0.0.  Group coordination is working as expected, with each message being processed by one consumer in the group, however (n-1)/n consumers in the group are running at 100% CPU.  With n=1 CPU load is low as expected, however with n>1 I see n-1 processes running 100% CPU as if they were busy-spinning.

I'm seeing this with the following, stripped-down consumer worker function:

```python
def consumer_worker(kafka_uri, topic, group_id, heartbeat, stats_array):
    consumer = kafka.KafkaConsumer(topic, group_id=group_id, bootstrap_servers=[kafka_uri])
    for message in consumer:
        print "consumer_worker iterating"
```

The print shows that the for isn't busy-spinning.  I have not performed any further profiling at this point.

issue_title: AttributeError: 'Session' object has no attribute 'known'
issue_body:
Hi,

After upgrading to version 0.42.0, my rom queries regularly crash with the following stacktrace:

```python
    return query.all()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/rom/query.py", line 688, in all
    return self.execute()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/rom/query.py", line 682, in execute
    return list(self)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/rom/query.py", line 488, in _iter_results
    isk = set(session.known.keys())
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/eventlet/corolocal.py", line 45, in __getattribute__
    return object.__getattribute__(self, attr)
AttributeError: 'Session' object has no attribute 'known'
```

Looking at what changed in 1dade8515f5efd5415f6e5e9e0f6bb1e5136694a it seems like it uncovers a bug that was already there previously with the attribute `known` of the class `rom.util.Session` not always being initialised.

It is initialised in `rom.Session._init()`, which is only called by `Session.add()`, `.forget()`, `.get()`, `.flush()` and `.refresh()`.  
So in the scenario where a query with filters is the first thing executed on the session, before any of those operations, `.known` does not exist, hence the statement `isk = set(session.known.keys())` (added in 1dade8515f5efd5415f6e5e9e0f6bb1e5136694a) crashes with the above exception.

Is there any reason for not systematically running that `_init()` method in `__init__()`?

issue_title: read_series() incompatible with DICOM from (3T) Philips MRI
issue_body:
It seems that pydicom `read_series()` is incompatible with DICOM produced by Philips MRI.

The `DicomSeries` class has a `_finish()` method that is called on every series found by `read_series()`. However, the `_finish()` method pause some heuristics on the internal DICOM data structure that are incompatible with Philips DICOM. For instance,

  > ds.PixelSpacing

and

  > ds.ImagePositionPatient

doesn't exist as-is in Philips DICOM. It is instead

  > ds.PerFrameFunctionalGroupsSequence[n].PixelMeasuresSequence[0].PixelSpacing

and

  > ds.PerFrameFunctionalGroupsSequence[n].PlanePositionSequence[0].ImagePositionPatient

where `n` is a valid frame number.

The series is never returned by the `read_series()` method because it is not considered a volumetric dataset, even if it is.

issue_title: Add support for DICOM datasets in single file ZIP containers
issue_body:
It would be very useful for me to have built-in support for DICOM datasets in single file ZIP containers.

These ZIP containers I am very often working with contain only one DICOM dataset (with the file meta header).
It would be great to have built-in support for this in pydicom.
This could then be used in my project, pydicomtools.

I can probably provide a pull request for this myself. Currently I am thinking of just checking for the magic bytes of the ZIP header ('PK') and then using python's zipfile module to read the data.

issue_title: Determining ambiguous VRs in implicit data sets
issue_body:
With datasets that have an implicit transfer syntax, the 'SmallestImagePixelValue' and 'LargestImagePixelValue' elements will get a VR of "US or SS".  However, in this case we could figure out the precise VR by looking at the value of 'PixelRepresentation' to see if the pixel data is signed or unsigned.

I was looking through the code to make a pull request, but it isn't clear to me how to go about it.  Currently, the conversion of each raw data element is handled completely independently with no real opportunity to examine the rest of the data set and make this decision.  Any advice?

issue_title: Heads-up about the new PyPI
issue_body:
Hi! I saw your [blog post](https://circleci.com/blog/continuously-deploying-python-packages-to-pypi-with-circleci/). I'm the project manager for the new Python Package Index (Warehouse), which is currently in pre-production at http://pypi.org/ . On [the Warehouse roadmap](https://wiki.python.org/psf/WarehouseRoadmap), it looks like the full switch will happen sometime in April, so here's a heads-up about why we're switching, what's changed, and what to expect. (A bunch of this is not directly relevant to circleci.py but I thought you might want to know anyway!)

The legacy PyPI site at https://pypi.python.org started in the early 2000s. In recent years, users faced outages, malicious packages, and spam attacks, and the legacy codebase made it hard to maintain and even harder to develop new features.

The new PyPI has a far more modern look, and is up-to-date under the hood as well; a proper web framework (Pyramid), 100% backend test coverage, and a Docker-based development environment, make it easier for current and new developers to maintain it and add features.

Thanks to [Mozilla's Open Source Support funding](https://pyfound.blogspot.com/2017/11/the-psf-awarded-moss-grant-pypi.html), developers have added many new features, overhauled infrastructure, and made steady progress towards redirecting traffic to the new site and shutting down the old one. As of the middle of last year, package releases must go through the new PyPI, and as of late February, new user account registration is only available on the new site (so it would be great if you could update your post's PyPI registration link to point to https://pypi.org/account/register/ ). The full switch will include redirecting browser and `pip install` traffic from the old site; then, sometime in late April or early May, the legacy site will be entirely shut down.

Your wrapper will probably be able to seamlessly switch to the new site, and thanks to redirects, may not have to change anything immediately. [Here's a migration guide.](https://warehouse.readthedocs.io/api-reference/integration-guide/#migrating-to-the-new-pypi)

Some new PyPI features:

* mobile-responsive UI
* chronological release history for each project ([example](https://pypi.org/project/pip/#history))
* easy-to-read project activity journal for project maintainers
* better search and filtering
* support for multiple project URLs (e.g., [for a homepage and a repo](https://packaging.python.org/tutorials/distributing-packages/#project-urls))
* user-visible Gravatars and email addresses for maintainers
* no need to "register" a project before initial upload
* far better backend infrastructure, reducing the frequency of outages

Things that are going away, or already have (sometimes for policy or spam-fighting reasons), include:

* pythonhosted.com documentation hosting (pypa/warehouse#582)
* [download counts visible in the API](https://warehouse.readthedocs.io/api-reference/xml-rpc/#changes-to-legacy-api) (instead, use [the Google BigQuery service](https://packaging.python.org/guides/analyzing-pypi-package-downloads/))
* GPG/PGP signatures for packages (still visible in the [Simple Project API](https://warehouse.readthedocs.io/api-reference/legacy/#simple-project-api) per [PEP 503](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0503/), but no longer visible in the web UI
* key management: PyPI no longer has a UI for users to manage their GPG or SSH public keys
* package maintainers being able to upload a new release via the web UI (instead, the recommended command-line tool is [Twine](http://twine.readthedocs.io/))
* package maintainers being able to log in and update release descriptions via the web UI (to update release metadata, they need to upload a new release; see [distutils-sig discussion](https://mail.python.org/pipermail/distutils-sig/2017-December/031826.html))
* [OpenID and Google auth login](https://mail.python.org/pipermail/distutils-sig/2018-January/031855.html)
* users being able to upload a package without verifying their email address with PyPI first
* [HTTP access to APIs; now it's HTTPS-only](https://mail.python.org/pipermail/distutils-sig/2017-October/031712.html)

And in the works:

* [PEP 541](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0541/) will enable more timely package takeovers, as people get package names transferred to them after conflict resolution
* Now that PEP 566 has been approved, [developers are working to get Markdown supported for README files on PyPI](https://github.com/pypa/warehouse/issues/869#issuecomment-340928703)

For future updates, please sign up for [the low-traffic PyPI announcements email list](https://mail.python.org/mm3/mailman3/lists/pypi-announce.python.org/).

Thank you for integrating with PyPI, and please [let us know](https://github.com/pypa/warehouse/issues/new) if you have any questions or problems with the new site!

issue_title: MSSQL: string with emoji is truncated on insert
issue_body:
When inserting a string containing emoji characters into a `NVARCHAR` column in MSSQL using FreeTDS 1.00.26 + unixODBC 2.3.4 + Python 3.5 + pyodbc 4.0.15 and the default connection properties (as per recommendation in the documentation):

```
text = 'Test 1 2 3 😂  foo bar'

cursor.execute("CREATE TABLE dbo.unicode_test (val NVARCHAR(100));")

cursor.execute("INSERT INTO dbo.unicode_test (val) VALUES (?);", (text,))

cursor.execute("SELECT TOP (1) val FROM dbo.unicode_test;")

print(cursor.fetchone())
('Test 1 2 3 😂  foo ba',)
```

As you can see, the final "r" is dropped. For each extra emoji in the text, more characters seem to be truncated (N emoji = last N characters are dropped).

This seems to happen when pyodbc is converting the UTF8 string when executing the INSERT statement, since the database does not contain the final character (I checked this with other tools). 

When I convert the text to UTF-16-LE using `text.encode('utf_16_le')`, there is no truncation.

issue_title: A means to override user-agent?
issue_body:
It seems some Insight providers are blocking API requests by looking at the user-agent. For example, a request like this to `localbitcoinschain.com`:

```
curl -A Python-urllib/3.5 https://www.localbitcoinschain.com/api/addr/1PBDnk9JdQ4ckwyM5RPMsULegYo8C8W7k4/utxo
```
... will fail with 403 from Cloudflare, but ...

```
curl -A whatever https://www.localbitcoinhain.com/api/addr/1PBDnk9JdQ4ckwyM5RPMsULegYo8C8W7k4/utxo
```

... works fine.

IMHO, it would be appropriate to put "pycoin/0.71" as the user agent for the requests coming from this library. However, if that's done, then it should be done for all the different providers.

`valid format`.

issue_title: How to get accented chars to group with their unaccented versions?
issue_body:
I am writing a PyQt app and am unhappy with the performance of their table sorting. However it does do "locale-aware" sorting in what I believe to be the correct way. Given this word list:

    words = ['apple', 'åpple', 'Apple', 'Äpple', 'Epple', 'Èpple', 'épple', 'epple']

and not ignoring case, Qt sorts in the order: apple, Apple, åpple, Äpple, epple, Epple, épple, Èpple

That is, all forms of A are grouped, then all forms of E. When I do the same sort in native Python using natsort:

    if locale.setlocale(locale.LC_ALL) == 'C' :
        locale.setlocale(locale.LC_ALL,'en_US.UTF-8')
    key_func_L = natsort.natsort_keygen( alg = natsort.ns.LOCALE )
    print( sorted( words, key=key_func_L )  )

The resulting order is 'Apple', 'Epple', 'apple', 'epple', 'Äpple', 'Èpple', 'åpple', 'épple'

That is, all accented forms sort higher than un-accented forms. I am not so concerned that in the one, lowercase is first and the other, uppercase is first. I am concerned that in a long table, words starting with é may be hundreds of rows removed from words starting with e.

I am working in Python 3.4, PyQt5.4, Mac OS 10.10. Changing the locale to fr_FR and de_DE didn't make any difference.

issue_title: App.add_domain() returns unnecessary 422
issue_body:
This corresponds to a support ticket I've opened.

Using the latest pypi release for this package (3.3.0) I run the following:

```
import heroku3
from django.conf import settings

heroku_conn = heroku3.from_key(settings.HEROKU_KEY)

# returns error:
# <really-long-uuid-here> 422 Client Error: {"id":"invalid_params","message":"Domain already added to this app."}
heroku_conn.apps()['my-app'].add_domain('somedomainthatdefinitelydoesntexistonthisappyet.com')
```

It would appear as though it's submitting the domain addition request twice, because the domain is added to the app as specified. So the first request adds the domain and returns a 200-level status, but the duplicate request returns 400 and raises an error within my app logic. Unfortunately I can't just catch and ignore this error because in the event an actual duplicate domain is submitted my app logic needs to handle the error gracefully.

See [App.add_domain](https://github.com/martyzz1/heroku3.py/blob/master/heroku3/models/app.py#L168)

issue_title: Add support for Registro.BR WHOIS source
issue_body:
The Brazil NIR "NIC.BR" (the exclusive source for resources in Brazil, by delegation from LACNIC) does not have a traditional IRR. Instead a WHOIS database is used which in concept is similar to ARIN-WHOIS, but in terms of quality seems to be more accurate.

Registro.br (under the umbrella of NIC.BR) publishes a computer parse-able dump of the WHOIS registry which can be consumed to construct BGP filters.

The dump is published every weekday here: ftp://ftp.registro.br/pub/numeracao/origin/nicbr-asn-blk-latest.txt

the format is as following, a like like this:

```
AS6543|Ecospar Serv. e Part. Ltda.|35.809.342/0001-31|2804:69c::/32|200.219.0.0/21
```
can be transformed to the following IRR format:

```
route6: 2804:69c::/32
origin: AS6543
source: REGISTROBR

route: 200.219.0.0/21
origin: AS6543
source: REGISTROBR
```

issue_title: extract_binds with imports
issue_body:
I'm trying to use a variable extracted from the headers through my tests. My main .yaml file is simply:

```
---
- config:
    - testset: "App tests"

- import: test/auth.yaml
- import: test/items.yaml
```

Both imported files use the same test set, and the lifecycle docs say:

> Parse YAML, reading top-level imports and building TestSets

and also:

> Generate a Context **for each test set**, populated with generators and variables defined in the TestConfig

And:

> Contexts are persistent within a TestSet. Once a variable is set, it can be used in all following tests

But a variable declared in the first import isn't available in the second. I've moved the tests from the second import into the first import and it all works perfectly, so the variable is definitely being set correctly. 

Example from `auth.yaml`:

```
---
- config:
    - testset: "App tests"

- test:
    - group: "Authentication"
    - name: "User successful login"
    - url: "/api/v0.1/auth/"
    - method: "POST"
    - body: '{"username": "xxxx", "password": "xxxx", "pin": xxxx }'
    - headers: {"Content-Type": "application/json"}
    - expected_status: [200]
    - validators:
      - json_schema: {schema: {file: "auth-schema.json"}}
    - extract_binds:
      - "auth_key": {"jsonpath_mini": "token"}
```

And then `items.yaml`:

```
---
- config:
    - testset: "App tests"

- test:
    - group: "Items"
    - name: "User item listing successful"
    - url: "/api/v0.1/items/?limit=10"
    - method: "GET"
    - headers: { template: {"Api-Key": "$auth_key", "Content-Type": "application/json"}}
    - expected_status: [200]
```

Do I need a config section in each imported file? Adding or removing it seems to make no difference to how the tests run.

The item listing call using verbose mode shows:

`Api-Key:$auth_key`

issue_title: Override
issue_body:
While working on extending the test coverage, I noticed that the `override` parameter of `.copy` didn't actually do anything; it deleted the file before copying to the new location, but the underlying copy operation copied even if the file existed, so it was meaningless. I've fixed it to throw an error in the pytest branch, but that raises a further question: we should support a single method for choosing what is done on copy/move/etc, so should it be: 

* an override parameter, that can be `True`, `False`, or `None`, throwing an error if `False`, and silently ignoring the failed copy/move if `None`. The default would be `True`, since the current `False` acts like `True`. (Open to discussion!). This would be uniformly implemented on all copy and move functions.
* Other suggestions?

issue_title: issue with hg bookmarks
issue_body:
Apparently hg bookmarks are also seen as tags, which results in the following version string:
```
$ hg log -r . --template '{latesttag}-{latesttagdistance}-hg{node|short}'
default/master-1-hga61ca3b970ea
```
A more helpful default hg command would be:
```
$ hg log -r . --template "{latesttag('re:^v[0-9\.]+((a|b|rc)[0-9]*)?$') % '{tag}-{distance}'}-hg{node|short}"
v1.0a6-38-hga61ca3b970ea
```
I was going to override it in setup.py, only then I noticed I would lose the ability to also use git, and that is a dealbreaker since I use both hg and git. Would you be open to a pull request that changes the default, or maybe allows you to overwrite default commands for hg or git exclusively?

issue_title: Document save func says to use .path() instead of .patch()
issue_body:
save(waitForSync=False, **docArgs)[source]
Saves the document to the database by either performing a POST (for a new document) or a PUT (complete document overwrite). If you want to only update the modified fields use the **.path()** function. Use docArgs to put things such as ‘waitForSync = True’ (for a full list cf ArangoDB’s doc). It will only trigger a saving of the document if it has been modified since the last save. If you want to force the saving you can use forceSave()

issue_title: Support some sort of method to exclude glyphs from instances.
issue_body:
In Superpolator it is possible to exclude specific glyphs from an instance. The glyphs are present in all calculations and festivities, they're just not written to the final UFO. For instance glyphs that play a role in rules.

Currently in MutatorMath's designspace format, when no further information is given, it is assumed all glyphs need to be generated. The only way to exclude glyphs is then to write all but one glyphs in the instance. This would add a lot of data to the .designspace file.

**Proposal**: an option that accepts a list of glyph names, and adds an element to the instance record that specifically excludes that glyph.

```xml
    <instances>
        <instance familyname="Bla" filename="Bla-Awesome.ufo" stylename="SoAwesome">
            <location>
                <dimension name="width" xvalue="394.344554" />
            </location>
            <!-- this would generate all glyphs except the 'm' -->
            <glyph name="m" ignore="1">
        </instance>
    </instances>

```

issue_title: Removing support for redundant database connection parameters
issue_body:
Right now, the MongoDB storage adapter and the SQL storage adapter each support both of the following parameters.

- `database`: A simple string such as "chat-bot-database" that then gets turned into a database connection string by the storage adapter.
- `database_uri` A database connection string.

I think it might simplify things if one of these parameters was removed. Considering they both almost do the same thing I don't think there would be too much of an issue if `database` was removed completely as a possible parameter for these two adapters.

***

> "Explicit is better than implicit."
> ~ [The Zen of Python](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0020/)

issue_body:
The method ```def _get_bbox(self, south, west, north, east):``` in ```base.py``` sometimes raises an error like:

```python
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "C:\Python27\lib\site-packages\geocoder\api.py", line 114, in google
    return get(location, provider='google', **kwargs)
  File "C:\Python27\lib\site-packages\geocoder\api.py", line 100, in get
    return options[provider][method](location, **kwargs)
  File "C:\Python27\lib\site-packages\geocoder\google.py", line 70, in __init__
    self._initialize(**kwargs)
  File "C:\Python27\lib\site-packages\geocoder\base.py", line 98, in _initialize
    self._json()
  File "C:\Python27\lib\site-packages\geocoder\base.py", line 104, in _json
    value = getattr(self, key)
  File "C:\Python27\lib\site-packages\geocoder\google.py", line 122, in bbox
    return self._get_bbox(south, west, north, east)

    self.northeast = [self.north, self.east]
AttributeError: 'Google' object has no attribute 'north'
```

I think that the google module sometimes does not return proper results. This happend for calls like ```g = geocoder.google('<city>')``` and ```g = geocoder.google([<lat>, <lng>])```. Calls like ```g = geocoder.google([<lat>, <lng>], method='reverse')``` seem to work fine. So I think it is necessary to strengthen the error handling in that method.

If you want I can try to fix this in the next days. Just let me know.

issue_title: Accept verbosity option while creating Django backend
issue_body:
`PostmarkClient` accepts a `verbosity` option. However, there is no way to specify this option when using the Django backend. My workaround is to add a `VERBOSITY` field in `settings.POSTMARK` and use this field's value in my custom backend. It would be good to have this functionality in `postmarker`.

```python
from postmarker.core import PostmarkClient
from postmarker.django import EmailBackend as PostmarkerDjangoEmailBackend

class EmailBackend(PostmarkerDjangoEmailBackend):
    def open(self):
        if self.client is None:
            verbosity = self.get_option('VERBOSITY') or 0
            self.client = PostmarkClient(token=self.token, verbosity=verbosity)
        return False
```

issue_title: Adding formulas is now broken
issue_body:
I have some columns that generate formulas for Google sheets. Since the new version, these are now broken since they are now sent as 'USER_ENTERED'. I had to do this to work around non-JSON serializable items, allowing me to stringify them first.

The two ways to fix this are:
- Create a better way to serialize objects, or stringify only when needed (seems complicated, error prone, and hard to handle dates)
- Leave as is and add an option to pass specific columns as formulas (seems like the better option, but requires a little more effort on the user's part)

issue_title: Support disabling pylint via the command line
issue_body:
Hey, thank you for this package! I have a small feature request - we add `--pylint` to our `setup.cfg` file so that it is always run alongside our tests. This works fine, but I find myself wishing to just run our tests locally without the linting (which is a bit faster). 

I'd love the ability to run `pytest --no-pylint` and have it override the auto-included `--pylint` flag, so that `pytest --pylint --no-pylint` will not run pytest.

issue_title: Reverting two levels deep with ForeignKeys
issue_body:
I can revert with `ForeignKey` relationships, however, I was wondering if it's possible to do so where there are two levels of `ForeignKey`'s

For instance, based on the pseudocode below:

```
class Book(models.Model):
    name = CharField(... ...)

reversion.register(Book, follow=['chapters'])

class Chapter(models.Model):
    book = ForeignKey(Book, related_name='chapters')
    name = CharField(... ...)

reversion.register(Chapter, follow=['pages'])

class Page(models.Model):
    chapter = ForeignKey(Chapter, related_name='pages')
    num = IntegerField(... ...)

reversion.register(Page)
```

- [x] I **can** delete and revert a `Book` instance, fully recovering its underlying `Chapter` instances
- [x] I **can** delete and revert a `Chapter` instance, fully recovering its underlying `Page` instances
- [ ] I would like to, but **can NOT** delete a `Book` instance, fully recovering both its underlying `Chapter` instances, as well as the underlying `Page` instances under each of the `Chapter`'s
        
Is this possible? If not, would you be open to a pull request with this feature?

issue_title: 0.4.3 在取大于800支股票的时候崩溃
issue_body:
下列代码会导致崩溃。测试环境为aiohttp 1.0.5

```
import easyquotation as eq
sina = eq.use('sina')
print(sina.all)
```

原因可能是ClientSession的误用，aiohttp的文档中描述了，不应该为每一个请求建立一个session，如URL:

http://aiohttp.readthedocs.io/en/stable/client.html

而0.4.3版的easyquotation的代码在get_stocks_by_range函数中正是这样做的，因此会导致如果请求大于800支股票，产生多个routines的时候崩溃。

一个可能的解决方法是把session设为成员变量。如下：
```
    async def get_stocks_by_range(self, params):
        headers = {
            'Accept-Encoding': 'gzip'
        }
        async with self._session.get(self.stock_api + params, timeout=5, headers=headers) as r:
            response_text = await r.text()
            return response_text

    def get_stock_data(self, stock_list):
        coroutines = []
        self._session = aiohttp.ClientSession()
        for params in stock_list:
            coroutine = self.get_stocks_by_range(params)
            coroutines.append(coroutine)
        try:
            loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
        except RuntimeError:
            loop = asyncio.new_event_loop()
            asyncio.set_event_loop(loop)
        res = loop.run_until_complete(asyncio.gather(*coroutines))

        self._session.close()
        return self.format_response_data(res)

```

issue_title: py.test ImportMismatchError failure
issue_body:
== tldr; ==

When running python setup.py pytest I get this error:
```
====================================================== ERRORS =======================================================
______________________________________ ERROR collecting jaraco/collections.py _______________________________________
/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/py/_path/local.py:668: in pyimport
    raise self.ImportMismatchError(modname, modfile, self)
E   ImportMismatchError: ('jaraco.collections', '/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/jaraco/collections.py', local('/srv/git/irclib-update/python-jaraco-collections/jaraco.collections-1.3.1/jaraco/collections.py'))
========================================= 1 passed, 1 error in 0.04 seconds =========================================
```
It seems to be triggered by having a version of jaraco.collections (in this case, the same version as I'm trying to build) installed and attempting to run the test suite in the source.

== Little more info ==

jaraco.collections and jaraco.text have a dependency on each other.  So the distro package for jaraco.text that I've built Requires: jaraco.collections.  This pulls in the distro package of jaraco.collections.  For someone installing the packages on a system this works fine.

When I want to run the test suite from within a package build of jaraco.collections I need to have the dependencies of the packages installed.  So I list jaraco.text as a BuildRequires.  This installs the distro package of jaraco.text (good!) but since that requires jaraco.collections, the distro package of jaraco.collections is also installed.

Then when I run the tests via pytest, pytest finds both the distro package and the local package and emits that ImportMismatchError.

I'm not sure why pytest is finding both versions -- I've tried setting PYTHONPATH to the directory I'm building jaraco.collections in to try to have the local version picked up first but that has no effect.  I have found an ugly, ugly workaround which I'm using to build and test the package for now: in the toplevel directory,
```
mv jaraco/ j/
python2 setup.py pytest
mv j/ jaraco/
```

I'd love to get rid of that hack but I'm not sure what py.test is really complaining about or how to really fix it.

issue_title: Compile error when broadcasting
issue_body:
I'm running into an issue when broadcasting inside a pythran function a minimal example is below:

```python
import numpy as np

#pythran export  testfail(complex128 [], float [][], complex128 [], int)
def testfail(E, testangles, symbols, N):
     comp_angles = np.exp(1.j * testangles)
     Eangles = E[:, np.newaxis] * comp_angles
     dd = Eangles[:,:,np.newaxis] - symbols
     return dd
```

this results in a compile error with both gcc and clang. 
[gcc.log](https://github.com/serge-sans-paille/pythran/files/1998960/gcc.log)
[clang.log](https://github.com/serge-sans-paille/pythran/files/1998961/clang.log)

I can work around the error by putting the second newaxis subtraction into an explicit loop, however this is very slow  compared to unwrapping all loops. Example code below:
```python
import numpy as np

#pythran export  testworks(complex128 [], float [][], complex128 [], int)
def testworks(E, testangles, symbols, N):
     comp_angles = np.exp(1.j * testangles)
     Eangles = E[:, np.newaxis] * comp_angles
     dd = np.zeros((Eangles.shape[0], Eangles.shape[1], symbols.shape[0]), dtype=E.dtype)
     for i in range(symbols.shape[0]):
         dd[:,:,i] = Eangles - symbols[i]
     return dd

#pythran export  testfaster(complex128 [], float [][], complex128 [], int)
def testfaster(E, testangles, symbols, N):
     comp_angles = np.exp(1.j * testangles)
     Eangles = E[:, np.newaxis] * comp_angles
     dd = np.zeros((Eangles.shape[0], Eangles.shape[1], symbols.shape[0]), dtype=E.dtype)
     for j in range(Eangles.shape[0]):
         for k in range(Eangles.shape[1]):
            for i in range(symbols.shape[0]):
                dd[j,k,i] = Eangles[j,k] - symbols[i]
     return dd
```

```python
In [1]: import ptest2

In [2]: s=np.random.rand(10**4)+1.j*np.random.rand(10**4)

In [3]: s2=np.random.rand(40)+1.j*np.random.rand(40)

In [4]: a=np.linspace(0, np.pi/2, 50).reshape(1,-1)

In [5]: timeit ptest2.testworks(s, a, s2, 10)
407 ms ± 3.34 ms per loop (mean ± std. dev. of 7 runs, 1 loop each)

In [6]: timeit ptest2.testfaster(s, a, s2, 10)
134 ms ± 864 µs per loop (mean ± std. dev. of 7 runs, 10 loops each)

In [7]: 
```
this seems to scale with the size of s2 and the difference also becomes larger when enabling compiler flags (i did not use any here).

issue_title: Document accepted types for `data` argument to *write methods
issue_body:
The Pydoc for USBDeviceHandle.interruptWrite (and the other handle write methods) lists the `data` parameter but makes no mention that integers are not allowed (intentionally or not). Each write method calls `create_binary_buffer` with their write data. If an int is passed in, create_binary_buffer returns an empty buffer of length `data`.  

    bytesWrote = handle.interruptWrite(ENDPOINT, 0xf) # writes 15 bytes of \0
    bytesWrote = handle.interruptWrite(ENDPOINT, '\x0f') # writes 0xf, 1 byte

Is this a bug? Or expected behavior? Either way I find surprising.

I can submit a pull request with doc changes if you like.

issue_body:
I've noticed a strange behaviour in the behaviour of the `conditional_probability_alive` method of the `ModifiedBetaGeoFitter` class. I'm not sure if it's a shortcoming in the model or implementation, a data issue or a mistake I'm making, but I'm hoping someone has seen this type of result before and can help me along.

The problem lies in the distributions I'm getting for the values of P(alive) across customers. The following plot illustrates the issue:
![screenshot from 2018-03-16 23-58-16](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/734695/37549237-f71d4194-2975-11e8-8f4b-51e8401118d8.png)

The fairly smooth behaviour for high values of P(alive) is intuitively understandable, but the bulge around P(alive)=0.3 and the sharp cutoff is unexpected. Inspection reveals that the bulge is composed of customers who have made a single purchase, since it disappears when plotting only repeat customers:
![screenshot from 2018-03-16 23-58-25](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/734695/37549243-fcbb16ee-2975-11e8-827c-e1f4e34447fa.png)

The cliff on the left side of the bulge is composed of customers who made their single purchase near the beginning of the observation period, and the customers get more recent as you move right within the bulge.

This distribution of P(alive) for one-time customers doesn't seem to me to be an accurate or useful representation of these customers' probability of being alive, but the issue crops up across datasets so I'm wondering if:

- There is anything incorrect or implausible about my interpretation
- This is a known issue with this type of model
- There are any known workarounds or fixes

Any other thoughts or suggestions would be very welcome.

issue_title: Table.truncate fails
issue_body:
Table.truncate() has a default parameter `mode=None`, which overrides the default of `''` in SQLAdapter.truncate(), and hence produces a query like:

    TRUNCATE TABLE TablenameNone

i.e. appending the `None` to the tablename. Of course that leads to a 

    ProgrammingError: (1146, "Table 'TablenameNone' doesn't exist")

Shouldn't the default in Table.truncate() be `''` as well then? Or does one have to specify a `mode=''` explicitly for Table.truncate()?

issue_title: create_replication target
issue_body:
I have ran into multiple issues while using create_replication. When passing create_replication two couchDB objects, the requests consistently failed. I noticed that the target url was formatted differently than the web UI. create_replication called "database_url" on the database object and it was formatted like so:

user:password@user.cloudant.com/my_sample_db

while the web UI formatted the url without the pre-appended user:password@:

user.cloudant.com/my_sample_db

I resolved the issue by passing in my own object with the url formatted like the web UI. Is there any reason as to why this would have made a difference?

issue_title: Question about Float16
issue_body:
There is no errors on my side. I just want to ask a question about float16/FP16. Based on the info in this presentation http://on-demand.gputechconf.com/gtc/2017/presentation/s7218-training-with-mixed-precision-boris-ginsburg.pdf, there are three variables related to FP16: training values storage, matrix-multiply accumulator and master weight storage. At least nvidia caffe have these variables. In FP16 mode, there are multiple combinations:

training values storage | matrix-multiply accumulator |    training name
FP16       |                        FP32                                    |       mixed precision training
FP16        |                       FP16                                     |      FP16 training

In both mixed precision training and FP16 training, master weight storage could be either FP32 or FP16. 

So there are 4 combinations in total when using FP16 data types.

The question is that does MXNet have similar variables? When mxnet use float16 dtype, which one in these 4 combinations are you using? Do you also support these 4 combinations?

Thanks,
Rengan

issue_title: Empty Poller's poll method does not make use of timeout
issue_body:
If there's no socket registered to the poller the zmq.Poller.poll() method would get into a busy loop.  It seems like instead of waiting for the specified time to return, the poll() call would just return immediately.   For example you can do something like:

```python
import zmq
import time

context = zmq.Context()
poller = zmq.Poller()
for i in xrange(3):
    t = time.time()
    poller.poll(1000)
    print('%.8f' % (time.time() - t))
```

 and you will get:

```
0.00001693
0.00000215
0.00000119
```

If you register a socket with the poller it would timed out correctly, but it should not get into such unexpected looping behavior in my opinion. 

I'm using conda build of python 2.7.10 with:

```
In [2]: zmq.zmq_version()
'4.0.5'

In [3]: zmq.__version__
'14.7.0'
```

issue_title: Re-read .sops.yaml for key rotation (-r)
issue_body:
Adding or removing a key within .sops.yaml and rotating keys does not add or remove keys from the encrypted file.

Is this intentional and if so, could there be a flag to change this behaviour instead of a full decrypt/encrypt?

Example:
```
# Create configuration file with some fingerprints
vi .sops.yaml

# Confirm fingerprint in configuration file
$ grep 4BF .sops.yaml
  - pgp: '4BF...'

# Create test file
$ sops tester.yaml

# Verify fingerprint is in file
$ grep 4BF tester.yaml
        fp: 4BF...

# Remove fingerprint from configuration file
vi .sops.yaml

# Verify fingerprint is removed from configuration
$ grep 4BF .sops.yaml

# Rotate keys 
$ sops -r -i tester.yaml

# Observe that fingerprint remains in file <-- unexpected?
$ grep 4BF tester.yaml
        fp: 4BF...

# Re-encrypt and verify fingerprint is removed
$ sops -d -i tester.yaml && sops -e -i tester.yaml && grep 4BF tester.yaml

issue_title: Wait or submit in greenio pool
issue_body:
A useful method for the ``greenio.GreenPool` could be ``wait_or_submit(...)``.
This method should wait for the function is we are already on a child greenlet, otherwise ``submit`` to the pool and return a future.

Therefore the method either return a synchronous result, when in a child greenlet, or an asynchronous one, when in the main greenlet.

issue_title: Allow to select test functions
issue_body:
Currently it is only possible to select modules to run during testing, for example:

    python setup.py test -a http.client

runs all tests from the ``http/test_client`` module.
We need to be able to specify the test function too:
     
     python setup.py test -a http.client.200_get

Should run all **test functions* ``test_200_get`` (if there is one Test class per module, it will run one test only).

issue_title: Cython + 'unexpected keyword argument' hack
issue_body:
I'm not sure if Luigi or Cython should be blamed, but here's the problem I found. As you know, we can find this snippet in worker.py in _run_get_new_deps:
```python
try:
    task_gen = self.task.run(tracking_url_callback=self.tracking_url_callback)
except TypeError as ex:
    if 'unexpected keyword argument' not in str(ex):
        raise
```
Although it's clearly a hack, it does work most of the time. The exception message in indeed: `got an unexpected keyword argument 'tracking_url_callback`"

When we cythonize our program though, the exception message becomes: `run() takes no keyword arguments`, which makes Luigi unable to do anything.

I could push a quick&dirty patch, but I prefer asking before because you might prefer to get rid of this hack instead.

issue_title: Update S3 client to use Boto3
issue_body:
AWS is moving from `boto` to `boto3` for its Python SDK. Boto3 includes high-level wrappers for simple uploads and downloads from local files with [S3Transfer](https://boto3.readthedocs.org/en/latest/reference/customizations/s3.html#module-boto3.s3.transfer). Besides the simpler interface, in my own experience I've seen these advantages with Boto3

- More stable downloads for big files
- Built-in support for multipart uploads
- Encryption with [KMS](https://aws.amazon.com/kms/)

Happy to contribute this when I have some time over the next few months, otherwise I thought I'd put it out there for others to pick up

issue_title: Issues stemming from unpinned botocore dependency.
issue_body:
## background

I opened this [issue](https://github.com/jazzband/pip-tools/issues/634) on another project. But it may, in fact, be an issue more appropriate for boto3.

Dependency trees in python are everyone's favorite hell, and many otherwise stable python libraries actually depart from their declared dependencies because those dependencies conflict. Pip is very forgiving, but its behavior when installing a set of conflicting requirements is either undefined or hard to specify (depending on how you look at it).

Relatively recent tooling like https://github.com/jazzband/pip-tools helps the situation by, largely relying on existing pypa tools, providing avenues for avoiding dependency conflicts without a high overhead. From my vantage point, tools like these are being adopted relatively widely and quickly.

## issue

Because boto3's other boto project dependencies are editable, pointing to HEAD of development branches, boto3 confounds ecosystem dependency tooling and makes controlling a dependency tree that includes boto3 difficult.

Consider someone working with the following dependency constraints:
    
```
boto3
botocore<1.8.0
```

How should I evaluate what versions of boto3 are compatible with botocore versions < 1.8.0?

Because boto projects **do** tag/release, there are many projects out there whose dependency trees include explicit or implicit constraints on boto project versions.

## proposal

Would boto3 consider explicitly pinning it's dependencies to other boto projects? I understand that that represents increased maintenance cost.

I have a JSON-based API that is often returning cached blocks of JSON as part of its responses. The majority of the the time generating responses for users was spent dumping large blocks of JSON into Python dicts, lists etc. only to immediately load them as part of a JSON response. To improve its performance I've written a [terrible hack](https://github.com/ckan/ckan/blob/7a6e8e768f2c16fce48f122b11e510d9b8f240ca/ckan/lib/lazyjson.py#L39-L59). Since #118 was fixed my terrible hack no longer works.

Instead of making my hack even worse, I would like to submit a change to simplejson that would let me hook the `_iterencode` step to produce my own raw JSON as part of the encoded result. Would you accept such a change, and how would you like to see it done?

issue_title: Wrong resolution upon 2nd pipenv install
issue_body:
##### Describe your environment

1. macOS 10.12.6
1. Python version: any python version, bug seen with 2.7 and 3.6
1. Pipenv version: 9.0.0

##### Expected result

Expecting `pipenv install` to yield same result consistently, in this particular case `setuptools 33.1.1` should be resolved (due to pex constraint)

##### Actual result

pipenv resolves `setuptools 33.1.1` on 1st installation, but not on 2nd

##### Steps to replicate

You can reproduce like so (in any folder, using a scratch one here).

First time around, everything looks good (pex wants `setuptools<34`), note the minor annoying question **"Did you mean `pexpect`?"**:

```
pipenv --rm ; rm -f Pipfile* ; pipenv install pex ; pipenv install pytest --dev ; pipenv graph
...
Did you mean pexpect? [Y/n]: n
Installing pex…
...
pex==1.2.15
  - setuptools [required: >=20.3,<34.0, installed: 33.1.1]
...
pytest==3.3.1
  - setuptools [required: Any, installed: 33.1.1]
...
```

Now delete venv and install again -> wrong setuptools is resolved:

```
pipenv --rm ; pipenv install ; pipenv graph
...
pex==1.2.15
  - setuptools [required: >=20.3,<34.0, installed: 38.2.4]
...
pytest==3.3.1
  - setuptools [required: Any, installed: 38.2.4]
...
```

Also note that `pipenv install` acted here as-if I state `--dev`... wasn't expecting `pytest` to be installed here (was expecting it to be installed if I stated `--dev`).

issue_title: pipenv --update fails with SemVer violation
issue_body:
Running `pipenv --update` (with pipenv 9.0.0) fails with an error like the following:

```
ValueError: 11.10.1.dev5 is not valid SemVer string
```

This is presumably due to the release of dev versions of v11.10.1 over the last few days. Multiple runs of the command will fail on different dev versions. (I believe the problem is that, according to [SemVer spec item 9](https://semver.org/#spec-item-9), pre-release data should be separated by a hyphen, not a dot; thus the above version should be `11.10.1-dev.5`.)

issue_title: ☂ general awesomeness for klein.Plating
issue_body:
Here are some things that should be added to Klein to make it really complete, which were left out of the initial implementation to keep it compact:

- [ ] Support for different types of templates
   - [ ] the ones from twisted; `XMLFile` and `XMLString` as implied by #89's basic prototype
   - [ ] HTML5Lib
- [ ] Deferred support, including some kind of solution for asynchronous JSON serialization
- [ ] More documentation, particularly for how to use widgets
- [ ] Something on the input side, for conveniently parsing JSON post bodies and handling form data submission - something that *doesn't* involve handling state in memory, by the way, since that doesn't scale.  (Is there even a way in werkzeug's routing syntax to extract a query arg as a python parameter, or doe sit only accept path segments?)
- [ ] `attr.s` support - specifically, automatically invoking `asdict` so applications can work entirely in terms of nice data structures and not manually decompose things into dicts and lists
- [ ] pluggable renderer support.  the example even has a great use-case: latitude and longitude are conventionally presented as "east" and "west", "north" and "south", not positive and negative numbers.  We may want to present an individual serializable value in a different way.  I think that the way to do this might be `@plating.renderer("blub") def blubrender(plated_data, request, tag): "..."` to stuff things into a specific renderer's element. Still not sure how to do composition / renderer libraries nicely, but at the very least you could write a function and then do `add_utility_renderers(my_plating)`
- [ ] datetime serialization (what even is the best practice for this in JSON)

issue_title: Failure to properly parse DS9 region files that contain RGB colors such as "#6a8"
issue_body:
Hi, 

There seems to be a bug with the parsing of DS9 regions if the color has a hash sign in it.  Here is an example:
```
import pyregion
badstring = 'fk5;ellipse(5:34:26.665,-5:26:16.18,6.62208",12.3556",345) # color=#6a8 width=4 text={4267-616 (-55)} tag={Fast blue}'
goodstring = badstring.replace('#6a8', '6a8')

badregion, =  pyregion.parse(badstring)
goodregion, = pyregion.parse(goodstring)

print('pyregion version', pyregion.__version__)
print('Bad region:', badregion.attr)
print('Good region:', goodregion.attr)
```
which gives me the following output 
```
pyregion version 2.0.dev275
Bad region: (['color'], {})
Good region: ([], {'width': '4 ', 'text': '4267-616 (-55)', 'color': '6a8', 'tag': {'Fast blue'}})
```

I tried the new `astropy.regions` and that did not show the same problem.   However, it doesn't seem to support masks yet, which I make extensive use of.  

I can work around the problem by filtering out the hash signs before parsing with pyregion, so it is not such a big deal. 

Will

issue_title: Morphomaths binary operations always return a np.array even when input is a np.array subclass
issue_body:
## Description
Most morphological operations behave as expected, returning an array of the same type as the input array and preserving attributes. This is not the case with morphological operations starting with `binary_` which always return a `np.array` instance, losing input attributes.

## Way to reproduce

```python
In [1]: import skimage, sys, scipy, numpy; print(skimage.__version__, scipy.__version__, numpy.__version__, sys.version_info)
0.13.0 0.19.1 1.13.1 sys.version_info(major=3, minor=6, micro=2, releaselevel='final', serial=0)

In [2]: import numpy as np
   ...: from skimage import morphology
   ...: 
   ...: class InfoArray(np.ndarray):
   ...:     """An array with some extra attributes.
   ...: 
   ...:     The extra attributes will also be present in views of the array.
   ...:     Adapted from https://docs.scipy.org/doc/numpy/user/basics.subclassing.html
   ...:     """
   ...: 
   ...:     def __new__(cls, array, **kwargs):
   ...:         obj = np.asarray(array).view(cls)
   ...:         # add the new attributes to the created instance
   ...:         for key, value in kwargs.items():
   ...:             setattr(obj, key, value)
   ...:         # Finally, we must return the newly created object:
   ...:         return obj
   ...: 
   ...:     def __array_finalize__(self, obj):
   ...:         if type(obj) is not np.ndarray:
   ...:             for key, value in vars(obj).items():
   ...:                 setattr(self, key, value)
   ...:                 

In [3]: binary_image = InfoArray(np.zeros((25,25)), info='some info')
   ...: type(binary_image), type(binary_image[8:15,7:32]), binary_image[8:15,7:32].info
   ...: 
Out[3]: (__main__.InfoArray, __main__.InfoArray, 'some info')

In [4]: closed = morphology.closing(binary_image)
   ...: type(closed), closed.info
   ...: 
Out[4]: (__main__.InfoArray, 'some info')

In [5]: opened = morphology.opening(binary_image)
   ...: type(opened), opened.info
   ...: 
Out[5]: (__main__.InfoArray, 'some info')

In [6]: binary_closed = morphology.binary_closing(binary_image)
   ...: type(binary_closed)
   ...: 
Out[6]: numpy.ndarray

In [7]: binary_opened = morphology.binary_opening(binary_image)
   ...: type(binary_opened)
   ...: 
Out[7]: numpy.ndarray
```

issue_title: Add optional `ITranslationDomain` factory for `registerTranslations`
issue_body:
In https://bugs.launchpad.net/zope.i18n/+bug/428882, Ethan Jucovy (ethan.jucovy@gmail.com) reported:

> It is useful to be able to override the default `zope.i18n.translationdomain.TranslationDomain` -- for example I have one override that always injects a certain value into the translation mapping, and another that prepends all new catalogs rather than appending them.
> 
> This is currently difficult without monkeypatching `zope.i18n.zcml.handler` or forking its `registerTranslations` function.
> 
> The attached patch adds an optional `factory` argument to both `registerTranslations` and `handler` so that custom ZCML directives can invoke `registerTranslations` with their choice of `ITranslationDomain`.
> 
> It might also be worth checking that the object returned by the provided `factory` implements (or can be adapted to?) `ITranslationDomain`, and preventing startup if it does not.
> 
> The `<registerTranslations/>` directive could also be extended with an optional `factory` parameter whose default is `zope.i18n.translationdomain.TranslationDomain`. This isn't necessary, though, and it's arguably cleaner to write new directives for particular overrides.

with patch: https://bugs.launchpad.net/zope.i18n/+bug/428882/+attachment/714923/+files/add_factory.diff

issue_title: Feedback on the Fetch spec additions
issue_body:
I'd like to provide some preliminary feedback on the [Fetch spec patch](https://github.com/google/brotli/blob/master/fetch-spec/shared-brotli-fetch-spec.txt).

First of all, thanks for working on Shared Brotli! I'm super excited about the possibilities :)

High level comments:
* The entire approach for the protocol would highly benefit from an explainer document detailing in high-level what are the use cases that the protocol tackles, what it looks like, how it works, etc.
* Integration of the protocol's processing with Fetch is great, but you probably also need an IETF draft to describe the headers and the mechanism. Also, Fetch implies that this protocol is only relevant to browsers, and doesn't cover other HTTP clients. Is that the case?

Practical comments:
* How does the server know what dictionaries the client has? AFAICT there's no mechanism for the client to publish available dictionaries for a certain domain. Also, depending on the use-case, publishing such a list may not be feasible.
* It's not immediately clear what's done with the SRI info. Is it just for validation purposes, or is it used for caching in some way?
* The parts about `recursive-sbr` are not clear. IIUC, that's used to update a dictionary from a previous dictionary. If so, why is `Accept-encoding: sbr` forbidden? How does the server know to provide an sbr resource in this case?

issue_title: BrotliDecompressBufferStreaming (/ BrotliDecompressStream) setting available_in to 1
issue_body:
Using brotli from master, sometime when I call BrotliDecompressBufferStreaming (/ BrotliDecompressStream), it sets available_in to 1. This is happening while the result code is BROTLI_RESULT_SUCCESS, so according to the documentation comment, this shouldn't be possible., and it's not clear if+how I should deal with this, since it appears to be a bug I'm guessing I can't make any assumptions as to there being any way to recover from this?

This didn't occur with a month-or-so older master brotli, but that had other issues (BrotliDecompressBufferStreaming always returning huge 4mb chunk sizes)

issue_title: Improve test suite coverage of lensing modules
issue_body:
Our three [worst-covered](https://codecov.io/gh/GalSim-developers/GalSim/tree/20142beef4301bfd37f1cd035b7f9c16d1d3c3f4/galsim) (non-wfirst) files are currently (in order) pse.py, lensing_ps.py, and nfw_halo.py.

This issue is to address that by
1. adding more tests to cover functionality that is currently insufficiently tested.
2. removing some functionality that we probably don't need.  I'm thinking especially of the 4 different return values for many functions in lensing_ps.py.  Probably 2 would suffice -- a single value or a numpy array probably covers all the relevant use cases.
3. adding `# pragma: no cover` for some branches that might not actually need coverage.

I think all three files are related enough that they can be done as a joint effort, but anyone who takes this on can feel free to only do one or two of the files and leave the rest for someone else.

issue_title: Cache chromatic drawImage intermediate results
issue_body:
This issue is to implement caches of intermediate products in the drawImage() and drawKImage() methods of `ChromaticObject` and subclasses where possible.

There are several instances where one may wish to call the `drawImage()` (or `drawKImage()`) method of a `ChromaticObject` with only minor changes between invocations, and in particular with no changes to `SED`s, `Bandpass`es, and chromatic PSFs.  Examples include forward-model fitting/sampling and ring tests.  In these cases, it would be advantageous to avoid recomputing certain intermediate results of `ChromaticObject.drawImage()` and `.drawKImage()`, such as the product of an SED and a Bandpass in the case of separable profiles, or the effective (wavelength-integrated) profile in the case of convolution of separable and inseparable profiles.

Previously, creating such a cache was problematic since it was difficult to recognize when particular GalSim objects had been seen before.  Now that (most) GalSim objects are immutable, though, and carry `__eq__` and `__hash__` methods, caching is feasible.  

My current plan is to steal the code for the cache from here: http://code.activestate.com/recipes/577970-simplified-lru-cache/ , but I'm happy to hear alternatives.

issue_title: sync standby not set when synchronous_mode_strict mode turned on
issue_body:
I've faced an issue with `synchronous_mode_strict` mode. 
I could reproduce issue under the following circumstances:
1. The first candidate after failover is the node with nosync tag.
2. The first candidate after failover is the Barman running pg_receivexlog.
When failover occurs Patroni sets `synchronous_standby_names = '*'` and sometimes PostgreSQL makes one of the potential standbys synchronous, even if it is Patroni member with nosync tag or Barman wal-receiver, while Patroni does not assign correct member to synchronous_standby_names (sometimes even when member without nosync tag is healthy).

Unfortunately I have not got enough debug information, and issue is not reproducable in 100% of cases. Can just provide some example.

Postgres: 9.6.9
Patroni: 1.4.6

Example 1
Initially:
```+---------+--------+----------------+--------------+---------+-----------+
| Cluster | Member |      Host      |     Role     |  State  | Lag in MB |
+---------+--------+----------------+--------------+---------+-----------+
|  pglab  |  pg0   | 89.113.131.148 |    Leader    | running |       0.0 |
|  pglab  |  pg1   | 192.168.10.112 | Sync standby | running |       0.0 |
|  pglab  |  pg2   | 192.168.10.113 |              | running |       0.0 | <- nosync tag
+---------+--------+----------------+--------------+---------+-----------+
```
```
[root@pglab1 ~]# patronictl show-config | grep synch
    synchronous_commit: remote_apply
synchronous_mode: true
synchronous_mode_strict: true
```
Action: restarting Patroni master node
```
| Cluster | Member |      Host      |  Role  |  State  | Lag in MB |
+---------+--------+----------------+--------+---------+-----------+
|  pglab  |  pg0   | 192.168.10.111 |        | running |       0.0 |
|  pglab  |  pg1   | 192.168.10.112 | Leader | running |       0.0 |
|  pglab  |  pg2   | 192.168.10.113 |        | running |       0.0 |
+---------+--------+----------------+--------+---------+-----------+
```
Result:
```
[root@pglab1 ~]# etcdctl get /service/pglab/sync
{"leader":"pg1","sync_standby":null}
```
```
postgres=# show synchronous_standby_names;
 synchronous_standby_names 
---------------------------
 *
(1 row)
```
```
postgres=# select usename, application_name, client_addr, backend_start, state, sync_priority, sent_location, write_location, sync_state from pg_stat_replication ;
  usename   | application_name |  client_addr   |         backend_start         |   state   | sync_priority | sent_location | write_location | sync_state 
------------+------------------+----------------+-------------------------------+-----------+---------------+---------------+----------------+------------
 replicator | pg2              | 192.168.10.113 | 2018-08-23 18:59:00.852169+03 | streaming |             1 | 6/840001A8    | 6/840001A8     | sync
 barman     | barman_pglab1    | 192.168.10.112 | 2018-08-23 18:59:03.498641+03 | streaming |             1 | 6/840001A8    | 6/840001A8     | potential
 barman     | barman_pglab2    | 192.168.10.113 | 2018-08-23 18:59:03.516632+03 | streaming |             1 | 6/840001A8    | 6/840001A8     | potential
 replicator | pg0              | 192.168.10.111 | 2018-08-23 18:59:12.602+03    | streaming |             1 | 6/840001A8    | 6/840001A8     | potential
```

pg1 patroni log:
```
Aug 23 18:58:51 pglab1 patroni: 2018-08-23 18:58:51,603 DEBUG: Writing {"leader":"pg1","sync_standby":null} to key /service/pglab/sync ttl=None dir=False append=False
Aug 23 18:58:51 pglab1 patroni: 2018-08-23 18:58:51,609 DEBUG: http://127.0.0.1:2379 "PUT /v2/keys/service/pglab/sync HTTP/1.1" 200 309
```

Action2: pg2 set nosync tag to false and reload:
```
Aug 23 19:03:48 pglab2 patroni: 2018-08-23 19:03:48,244 DEBUG: Writing {"conn_url":"postgres://192.168.10.113:5432/postgres","api_url":"http://192.168.10.113:8008/patroni","timeline":182,"state":"running","role":"replica","xlog_location":28017951088} to key /service/pglab/members/pg2 ttl=30 dir=False append=False
```
Result: pg2 officially became sync standby:
```
Aug 23 19:03:57 pglab1 patroni: 2018-08-23 19:03:57,777 DEBUG: Writing {"leader":"pg1","sync_standby":"pg2"} to key /service/pglab/sync ttl=None dir=False append=False
Aug 23 19:03:57 pglab1 patroni: 2018-08-23 19:03:57,785 DEBUG: http://127.0.0.1:2379 "PUT /v2/keys/service/pglab/sync HTTP/1.1" 200 309
```
```
 synchronous_standby_names 
---------------------------
 pg2
(1 row)
```

Example 2:
Initially:
```+---------+--------+----------------+--------------+---------+-----------+
| Cluster | Member |      Host      |     Role     |  State  | Lag in MB |
+---------+--------+----------------+--------------+---------+-----------+
|  pglab  |  pg0   | 192.168.10.111 |    Leader    | running |       0.0 |
|  pglab  |  pg1   | 192.168.10.112 | Sync standby | running |       0.0 |
|  pglab  |  pg2   | 192.168.10.113 |              | running |       0.0 | 
+---------+--------+----------------+--------------+---------+-----------+
```
Action: restart Patroni master node
Result: barman became synchronous node despite the fact pg2 is healthy.
```
postgres=# select usename, application_name, client_addr, backend_start, state, sync_priority, sent_location, write_location, sync_state from pg_stat_replication ;

(3 rows)

issue_title: Implement possibility of manual failover to specific node
Passing `requests` arguments through `get`
issue_body:
Hello,

it would be good if there was some way to passing arguments to `requests.get` in the `quandl.get` or in the `quandl.ApiConfig`. In particular, the argument `verify=False` which disables verification of the SSL certificate in HTTPS: some companies employ MitM in order to record traffic of employees for the compliance purposes, and in this case `quandl.get` does not work.

Best
Vladimir

issue_title: Ubuntu 18.04: FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: '/proc/4106/cwd'
issue_body:
Hello,

Guake crashes on the new Ubuntu 18.04 (Bionic) after closing a term.
Here is some info:

OS: Ubuntu 18.04 x86_64
Guake version: 3.0.5-1
Python version: 3.6.5
Gnome version: 3.28.1-1

Here is the error message:
`Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/guake/guake_app.py", line 1287, in accel_add
    self.add_tab()
  File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/guake/guake_app.py", line 1752, in add_tab
    pid = self.spawn_sync_pid(directory, box.terminal)
  File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/guake/guake_app.py", line 1667, in spawn_sync_pid
    wd = self.get_current_dir()
  File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/guake/guake_app.py", line 1643, in get_current_dir
    return active_terminal.get_current_directory()
  File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/guake/terminal.py", line 186, in get_current_directory
    cwd = os.readlink("/proc/{}/cwd".format(self.pid))
FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: '/proc/4106/cwd'
`

To reproduce:

1) Open a Guake term as normal user
2) Type the "exit" command. Guake hangs now.
3) Press the shortcut: CTRL + SHIFT + T. Guake crashes and the previous error message is send to Ubuntu.

When looking in /proc there is indeed no /proc/4106 dir.

from google.cloud.pubsub_v1.subscriber.policy import base

Exception in thread Consumer helper: consume bidirectional stream:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File ".../google/api_core/grpc_helpers.py", line 51, in error_remapped_callable
    return callable_(*args, **kwargs)
  File ".../grpc/_channel.py", line 342, in _next
    raise self
grpc._channel._Rendezvous: <_Rendezvous of RPC that terminated with (StatusCode.UNAVAILABLE, The service was unable to fulfill your request. Please try again. [code=8a75])>

The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:

    six.raise_from(exceptions.from_grpc_error(exc), exc)
  File "<string>", line 3, in raise_from
google.api_core.exceptions.ServiceUnavailable: 503 The service was unable to fulfill your request. Please try again. [code=8a75]

During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "${HOME}/.pyenv/versions/3.6.3/lib/python3.6/threading.py", line 916, in _bootstrap_inner
    self.run()
  File "${HOME}/.pyenv/versions/3.6.3/lib/python3.6/threading.py", line 864, in run
    self._target(*self._args, **self._kwargs)

    self._policy.on_exception(exc)

    self._future.set_exception(exception)

    raise RuntimeError('set_exception can only be called once.')
RuntimeError: set_exception can only be called once.
```

This is **PROGRESS**! It shows `67.833` seconds after the **first** `UNAVAILABLE`, a second one occurs and also tries to call `set_exception()`.

This is what remains after #4234.

issue_title: Find docstrings which could benefit from intersphinx
subtle bug in gcimagebuilder/utils.py xfs detection
issue_body:
Currently, line 97: `if self.type is 'xfs':`
should read `if self.type == 'xfs':`
and line 192: `if fs_type is 'xfs':`
should read: `if fs_type == 'xfs':`

The `is` operator explicitly compares object _identity_ rather than _value_ equality. Hat tip to @sjagoe for bug solution.

issue_title: Convert `invoke_runnable` to a general `call` and use to harden externs
issue_body:
The `invoke_runnable` extern is shaped like python `__call__`, and could replace a few other externs (`store_snapshot`, etc) with some minor tweaks.

Additionally, `project` and `project_multi` both invoke user code in many cases, meaning that they should really result in either success or failure like `invoke_runnable` does. Doing that would force failures to be handled, and implementing `project`/`project_multi` in terms of `invoke_runnable` would help with this.

issue_title: Implement `@rules` that will synchronously create all Subsystems
issue_body:
This is a prerequisite to #5788.

Before implementing the ability for an `@rule` to simply request a `Subsystem` inline (which involves Variants: see #5788), we should implement a series of `@rule`s like:
```
class OptionsParseRequest(datatype('cli_args', 'env', 'known_scopes', ..)): pass

@rule(Options, [Select(OptionsParseRequest)])
def parse_options(parse_request):
  ..
```
... which would do all relevant filesystem access required to parse configuration and compute all options relevant to the request.

The root `@rule` of this request might not survive #5788, but nearly everything else will, so this lays very important groundwork.
