issue_title: removing criteria breaks if shared set is empty + solutions
issue_body:
For googleads-python-lib/examples/adwords/v201502/advanced_operations/find_and_remove_criteria_from_shared_set.py  
The code provided currently breaks if it encounters a negative shared set of keywords that is empty, with no keywords or placements.  
This may or may not be a deliberate design decision to crash the program if it encounters a shared set that has no keywords or placements.
But for people who are using this code for more than just finding and removing criteria and/or people who wouldn't like their code to stop as soon as it encounters an empty shared sets, I have some fixes/workarounds for this behavior (that don't involve try). 

There are two reasons that this code throws errors and breaks when it encounters an empty shared set.  For each of the reasons, I have provided, solutions and/or workarounds to these problems, the probable cause of the program-stopping errors, as well as the error output.

Exhibit A: 
Problem:
When the code sets the initial values to look for criteria (line108), it sets totalNumEntries to be 1 in page.  This gets it into get into the loop looking at the result of page = shared_criterion_service.get(selector) (line 111) and then tries to look at page['entries'], where it then crashes, because page doesn't have 'entries' because the shared set it is looking at has no entries.  
error:
Printed below is the page(with a print page statement) for an empty shared set and the error that the code throws:
(SharedCriterionPage){
   totalNumEntries = 0
   Page.Type = "SharedCriterionPage"
 }
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "test6_add_gen_neg_keywords.py", line 423, in <module>
    newly_visited_shared_sets = main(adwords_client, campaign[0], visited_shared_sets, shared_set_table)
  File "test6_add_gen_neg_keywords.py", line 164, in main
    if page['entries']:
  File "/opt/devel/lib/python2.7/site-packages/suds/sudsobject.py", line 154, in __getitem__
    return getattr(self, name)
AttributeError: 'SharedCriterionPage' object has no attribute 'entries'
Solution:  
One solution (that I've implemented) is to check that the totalNumEntries is still more than 0 before trying to access page['entries'] in the line if page['entries']:
      if page['totalNumEntries'] > offset:
            if page['entries']: 
Another potential solution that I haven't tested is, instead of writing 
      if page['entries']:
at line 112 instead write 
      if 'entries' in page:
as written in line 79 of this code, since that page query doesn't break if a campaign doesn't have shared sets so this syntax seems to work.

Exhibit B:
Problem:
The code breaks at  response = shared_criterion_service.mutate(operations) (line 145)
if the operations doesn't have any criterion_ids to work on, aka if criterion_ids is an empty list.   I don't know why asking it to remove nothing breaks it, but I do have a solution. 
Error:
Printed below is the page for an empty shared set and the error given when trying to run this program
(SharedCriterionPage){
   totalNumEntries = 0
   Page.Type = "SharedCriterionPage"
 }
2015-06-23 13:40:08     suds.client ERROR    <suds.sax.document.Document instance at 0x1232ab8>
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "test6_add_gen_neg_keywords.py", line 423, in <module>
    newly_visited_shared_sets = main(adwords_client, campaign[0], visited_shared_sets, shared_set_table)
  File "test6_add_gen_neg_keywords.py", line 205, in main
    response = shared_criterion_service.mutate(operations)

issue_title: Release 0.7.0
issue_body:
Below are preliminary plans for things to do in release 0.7.0.

- [ ] remove support for old-style courses (Coursera shut down old courses anyway)
- [ ] remove `--ignore-http-errors` and make its behavior default
- [ ] implement quiz downloading and conversion to HTML

In release plans I usually prefer to put something that resembles features and visible changes. I don't put refactoring and bugfixes (except my huge ones).

@rbrito Another thing. I'm still thinking about https://github.com/coursera-dl/edx-dl/issues/77. I've registered mooc-dl package on PyPI. I hope we can put common code from coursera-dl and edx-dl there and quietly, under-the-hood, transition to it.

Let's not make loud statements but rather take small steps towards implementing it. I'm not sure how much time I can devote to it (new amazing courses on Coursera and EDX are coming up again and again) but at least let's make this first step. @rbrito Could you please create `mooc-dl` repository in this organization and grant me read-write access? Generally, I think youtube-dl and you-get projects should be inspiring for us and we should learn from them.

issue_title: Use DSC replicate number directly as seed for RNG
issue_body: In the current implementation, the default seed for RNG is `DSC_REPLICATE_ID + SCRIPT_ID` where `SCRIPT_ID` is the current `md5sum` hash of the script file, converted to long-int. But I'm a bit bothered by the fact that each time I change the script, the results change, because the seed changes ... I think we should just use `DSC_REPLICATE_ID` as the default. If users need fancier behavior they should just set their own seeds.

issue_title: No dest nat
issue_body: As a 4.x acos_client user, I'd like to be able to specify `no-dest-nat` on virtual server ports.

issue_title: Support all config options
issue_body:
As an a10 lbaas horizon user, command line user or scheduler author, I'd like to be able to use all of the configuration options for my devices.

Right now we only support the required device configuration options (`username`, `password`, `api_version`, and `host`) in the database of appliances. We should support all of our configuration options. If a custom scheduling hooks returned a new device config with optional configuration, we wouldn't store the optional configuration in the database and retrieve it when we find the device again.

 - [ ] Add all of the optional options to the database model, a new core migration
 - [ ] Add all of the optional options to the `A10ApplianceDB` model's `device(a10_context)` method that creates the device config
 - [ ] Add all of the optional options to the `Inventory` `device_appliance(device)` method that creates appliances for devices that don't already have one.
 - [ ] Add all of the optional options to the resource map for the api. These should be picked up by the command line client.
 - [ ] Add all of the optional options to an advanced expansion in the horizon ui for configuring images/instances.

issue_title: Add documentation for doing setup on OSX using virtualenv
issue_body:
Part of our setup guide needs to address virtualenvs for OSX users.
Because the system python on OSX is a battleground between the user's needs and the system's needs, it's not safe to rely upon.

More to the point: OSX ships with a stale version of `six`, which loads before any user-installed version.
To get around this, we should tell people to run in virtualenv.

issue_title: Support datetime[ns] in executemanycolumns
issue_body:
`cursor.executemanycolumns()` supports `datetime64[us]` and `datetime64[D]` columns (https://github.com/blue-yonder/turbodbc/blob/master/cpp/turbodbc_numpy/Library/src/set_numpy_parameters.cpp#L301). Support should be extended to `datetime64[ns]`, which is the type used by `pandas` for timestamp columns. This would allow pandas users to directly pass such columns as parameters without prior conversion to `datetime64[us]`.

This issue captures the essence of #112.

issue_title: Add support for non-standard packed mode
issue_body:
### What was wrong?

We should add support for non-standard packed mode as described in the solidity ABI spec here: https://solidity.readthedocs.io/en/develop/abi-spec.html#non-standard-packed-mode

### How can it be fixed?

I imagine support could be added in the form of another encoding function which could be imported by doing `from eth_abi import encode_packed` or something like it.  There is no need for a corresponding decoding function since the format is ambiguous and not parsable.

issue_title: Error switching `openssl` installs
issue_body:
We have observed a strange error when trying to switch from the `defaults` package of `openssl` to the `conda-forge` package of `openssl`. More details along with reproducing cases and logs can be see in issue ( https://github.com/conda-forge/openssl-feedstock/issues/3 ). It seems like maybe(?) it is using the cache incorrectly and misattributing it to a package from a different channel. It is unclear whether the latter is part of the problem that results in the installation failure or another related problem.

cc @msarahan @asmeurer

issue_title: conda allows '.' and '..' as environment names
issue_body:
**I'm submitting a...** - [x] bug report - [ ] feature request ## Current Behavior `conda` allows `.` and `..` as environment names. Since the environment names are `os.path.join`ed with an entry from `envs_dirs`, we can create some "useful" commands: ``` alias conda-nuke-envs='conda create -qyn. && conda remove -qyn. --all' alias conda-nuke-install='conda remove -qyn.. --all' ``` Surely there are always trivial ways to remove a Conda installation (`rm -rf "$(conda info --base)"` and such), but the above let `conda` itself remove those. BTW, if we wanted to handle obviously malicious ways (that still use `conda` to do the job) like ``` alias conda-nuke-conda='ln -s "$(conda info --base)" conda-root && conda remove -qyp conda-root conda ; rm conda-root' ``` , we would at least need to resolve symbolic links (`os.path.realpath`). ### Steps to Reproduce ``` (base) $ cd /opt/ (base) $ # let's make a backup that we can restore post-nuke :) (base) $ mv conda/ conda-bak (base) $ cp -R conda-bak/ conda (base) $ (base) $ alias conda-nuke-envs='conda create -qyn. && conda remove -qyn. --all' (base) $ alias conda-nuke-install='conda remove -qyn.. --all' (base) $ alias conda-nuke-conda='ln -s "$(conda info --base)" conda-root && conda remove -qyp conda-root conda ; rm conda-root' (base) $ (base) $ conda create -yqnsome-env && conda info --envs Solving environment: ...working... done ## Package Plan ## environment location: /opt/conda/envs/some-env Preparing transaction: ...working... done Verifying transaction: ...working... done Executing transaction: ...working... done # conda environments: # base * /opt/conda some-env /opt/conda/envs/some-env (base) $ conda-nuke-envs && conda info --envs Solving environment: ...working... done ## Package Plan ## environment location: /opt/conda/envs/. Preparing transaction: ...working... done Verifying transaction: ...working... done Executing transaction: ...working... done Remove all packages in environment /opt/conda/envs/.: ## Package Plan ## environment location: /opt/conda/envs/. # conda environments: # base * /opt/conda (base) $ (base) $ conda-nuke-install Remove all packages in environment /opt/conda/envs/..: ## Package Plan ## environment location: /opt/conda/envs/.. The following packages will be REMOVED: asn1crypto: 0.24.0-py36_0 ca-certificates: 2018.03.07-0 certifi: 2018.4.16-py36_0 cffi: 1.11.5-py36h9745a5d_0 chardet: 3.0.4-py36h0f667ec_1 conda: 4.6.0a2-py36_0 conda-canary conda-env: 2.6.0-h36134e3_1 cryptography: 2.2.2-py36h14c3975_0 idna: 2.6-py36h82fb2a8_1 libedit: 3.1.20170329-h6b74fdf_2 libffi: 3.2.1-hd88cf55_4 libgcc-ng: 7.2.0-hdf63c60_3 libstdcxx-ng: 7.2.0-hdf63c60_3 ncurses: 6.1-hf484d3e_0 openssl: 1.0.2o-h20670df_0 pip: 10.0.1-py36_0 pycosat: 0.6.3-py36h0a5515d_0 pycparser: 2.18-py36hf9f622e_1 pyopenssl: 17.5.0-py36h20ba746_0 pysocks: 1.6.8-py36_0 python: 3.6.5-hc3d631a_2 readline: 7.0-ha6073c6_4 requests: 2.18.4-py36he2e5f8d_1 ruamel_yaml: 0.15.35-py36h14c3975_1 setuptools: 39.1.0-py36_0 six: 1.11.0-py36h372c433_1 sqlite: 3.23.1-he433501_0 tk: 8.6.7-hc745277_3 urllib3: 1.22-py36hbe7ace6_0 wheel: 0.31.0-py36_0 xz: 5.2.3-h5e939de_4 yaml: 0.1.7-had09818_2 zlib: 1.2.11-ha838bed_2 bash: /opt/conda/bin/conda: No such file or directory (base) $ (base) $ mv conda-bak/ conda (base) $ conda-nuke-conda Solving environment: ...working... done ## Package Plan ## environment location: /opt/conda-root removed specs: - conda The following packages will be REMOVED: conda: 4.6.0a2-py36_0 conda-canary Preparing transaction: ...working... done Verifying transaction: ...working... done Executing transaction: ...working... done bash: /opt/conda/bin/conda: No such file or directory ``` ## Expected Behavior Maybe we just have to add a ```python if prefix_name in {'.', '..'}: from ..exceptions import CondaValueError raise CondaValueError( "'%(prefix_name)s' is not allowed as an environment name", prefix_name=prefix_name, ) ``` to https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/4.6.0a2/conda/base/context.py#L1145. Or are there more places to add checks? ## Environment Information

issue_title: Question on bcolz to numpy array conversion performance
issue_body:
I've being doing some benchmarks comparing different serialization formats and how fast we can read from disk. The comparison includes numpy's fromfile, hdf5 and bcolz:

https://gist.github.com/monkeybutter/ed72220be48333ce0dde

bcolz is amazingly fast in reading the file and creating the carray object, one order of magnitude faster than the second best, h5py. However, I would like to understand why the conversion of a bcolz to a numpy array is so expensive (gist cell no. 8)

I know bcolz is intended to be used to perform operations internally using the functionality it exposes, but to be fair with the benchmark I wanted to do the comparison using the same numpy array as the output for all the cases.

Is there anything that can be done to improve the speed of this conversion?

issue_title: [Enhancement] Add options for own preparsers
issue_body:
OCIDE calls cobc for compilation and conditional dbpre/esqlOC for specific files.
I'd like to see a more general approach being able to set up different preparsers (or even compilers) for specific file types.

Theses are the parts to consider:

* [optional, but nice] parser (to "know" about special sections/paragraphs that don't look like sections/paragraphs, and in the case I'd like to configure these have ";" in column 7 which may lead to not parse the files correctly) - can this part be "inserted" at runtime without the need to recompile the application at all?
* preferences - should be easily doable if the current SQL COBOL preference is changed to "external compilers" and has a list to choose the compilers, "hiding" the current text and file selection list until an entry is added/edited. the default would be only one entry for SQL COBOL (see below)


    Name: SQL COBOL
    Associated extensions: .sqb
    Compiler path: [empty, has to be filled with esql binary)
    Compiler flags: 
    [Checkbox] Only Preparser           Output file [only active if preparser is checked]:  [whatever you do currently]

If "Only Preparser" is checked the output file would be passed to cobc, if not the compiler either does the compilation on it's own or it calls cobc itself.

issue_title: Compiler setting: library and copybook paths need buttons for reordering
issue_body:
### Description

Earlier options take precedence. If you want to setup a new "look there first" you currently need to remove all entries, add your new one and add the other entries back.

This is a little disturbing if you somehow manage to have 12 entries already ...

issue_title: mock as runtime dependency
issue_body:
Hi,

When using 

az network public-ip create --resource-group xxx --name yyy

the code attempts to load the Python mock module, this appears to be counter intuitive. At runtime, i.e. when using the tools one would expect that things actually happen rather than actions/responses being mocked. Having a dependency on mock outside of testing appears strange.

File "/usr/lib/python3.4/site-packages/azure/cli/command_modules/network/custom.py", line 8, in <module>
    import mock

and in that code:

@mock.patch('msrestazure.azure_operation.AzureOperationPoller._poll', _poll)
def _generate_vpn_profile(....

@mock.patch('msrestazure.azure_operation.AzureOperationPoller._poll', _poll)
def _generatevpnclientpackage(....

@glaubitz please follow this as well

### Environment summary

openSUSE Leap 42.3 azure-cli packages

issue_title: acr says "Please verify if docker daemon is running properly." despite docker daemon running
issue_body:
**Problem**
After performing a login to the container registry: `az acr login --name <acrName>`
acr says "Please verify if docker daemon is running properly." despite docker daemon running.

**Suspected cause**
The root cause of this issue seems to be that [acr/custom.py](https://github.com/Azure/azure-cli/blob/master/src/command_modules/azure-cli-acr/azure/cli/command_modules/acr/custom.py) tries to run `docker ps` which needs elevated rights on some systems. So the command returns a non-zero error and throws an exception: `except CalledProcessError:`.

So a solution for the cli might be to provide a better warning.

A **workaround/solution** for people encountering this issue is to run the command with `sudo`:
`sudo az acr login --name <acrName>`

---

### Environment summary

Install Method: apt-get
azure-cli (2.0.22)
acr (2.0.16)

issue_title: ad: support az ad sp update --required-resource-access"
issue_body: CLI supports the flag on "az ad app create/update" command, but not on sp. This blocks managed service identity which hides the app object.

issue_title: Confusing/repetitive error message for invalid commands
issue_body:
Take a look at

```
# az foo
usage: az [-h] [--output {json,tsv,list}] [--verbose] [--debug]
          [--query JMESPATH]
          {storage,resource,network,role,redis,login,vmss,vm,ad,tag,webapp,account,logout,component,feedback,taskhelp}
          ...
az: error: argument _command_package: invalid choice: 'foo' (choose from 'storage', 'resource', 'network', 'role', 'redis', 'login', 'vmss', 'vm', 'ad', 'tag', 'webapp', 'account', 'logout', 'component', 'feedback', 'taskhelp')
```

you're basically repeating the same stuff at the `{...}` line as well as at the `az: error: argument _command_package: invalid choice` line.

Also zero users care about `argument _command_package`, so please try to get rid of it too.

issue_title: VM Create fails if default storage account chosen does not exist in the same location as the VM
issue_body: For default storage account, VM create takes the first storage account in the target resource group that is of the specified type. The portal allows you to choose any storage account in the same subscription and same location. If you have a storage account in the target resource group with the specified type _but the location differs from the VM_ the operation fails because it is unable to find the VM image.

issue_title: VM Create: if no ssh key is present, we should surface a friendly message to the user
Failure to upload large file (>200GB)
issue_body:
Hi guys, 

I have been having issue with uploading large file (>200GB) with the library. According to the [documentation](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/storageservices/understanding-block-blobs--append-blobs--and-page-blobs), with the REST API version after 2016-05-31, the size limit can be up to 4.75TB (100 MB chunk x 50000 blocks). 
I have been playing with the chunk size limit (default is 4MB). When I changed the MAX_BLOCK_SIZE to be larger than 10MB, i saw ReadTimeout
`AzureException: ReadTimeout: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='host.blob.core.windows.net', port=443): Read timed out. (read timeout=20)`
When the block size was 10MB and `validate_content=True`, I got md5 mismatched 
`AzureHttpError: The MD5 value specified in the request did not match with the MD5 value calculated by the server.`
When I kept the block size the same (10MB) and disable md5 check, the file got uploaded successfully (263GB). I downloaded the file down and manually performed md5sum on the whole file, the md5 matched with the file on local drive. 
I updated the library version to 0.36.0, but it didn't make any difference. 

Could you please take a look if this is a bug/service limit, or if I did not do it correctly?

Thanks

issue_title: Support for virtual adversarial perturbations
issue_body:
Foolbox currently only supports finding adversarial examples on samples that are correctly classified to begin with. If one tries to run an attack on an incorrectly classified sample, the following warning results:

>Not running the attack because the original image is already misclassified and the adversarial thus has a distance of 0.

However, in some contexts (such as Virtual Adversarial Training), it is useful to find adversarial examples on incorrectly classified inputs as well. That is, the training label is completely discarded and one instead only focuses on the label assigned by the classifier. These examples are called "virtual adversarial examples". Would it be possible to add support for them?

issue_title: Empty $TRAVIS_PYTHON_NAME included regardless of --env / -e
issue_body:
It seems (possibly due to a change on TravisCI), that ``$TRAVIS_PYTHON_NAME`` is currently empty, e.g.

https://codecov.io/gh/ipython/ipython/commit/c17ff28cb1220a91d94fc0dc4b85ccee561fb68f/builds

https://codecov.io/gh/biopython/biopython/commit/2d7619594003460b61fab62260ce50f6a344ba63/builds

Reading the code for ``--env`` aka ``-e``, the current behaviour is to automatically add some additional environment variables over-riding those specified at the command line, e.g. for TravisCI

``` python
include_env.add('TRAVIS_OS_NAME')
include_env.add('TRAVIS_%s_NAME' % language.upper())
include_env.add('TOXENV')
```

and for Snap:

``` python
include_env.add(os.getenv('DISPLAY'))
```

Having these as the default is nice (if ``-env`` or ``-e`` is not used), but to me having them over-ride the command line is surprising. As per #75 the documentation here could be clearer?

issue_title: From 7a04e74 to b07986f Attack_minimap not working
issue_body:
From Commit 7a04e74 (currently latest master branch commit as of 2018.05.06) to Commit b07986f (currently latest dev branch commit as of 2018.05.06), `actions.FUNCTIONS.Attack_minimap` doesn't work.  When called in my created agent as:
```python
return actions.FUNCTIONS.Attack_minimap('now', self.transformLocation(int(x) + (x_offset * 8), int(y) + (y_offset * 8)))
```
Once the agent hits the return, there is a long output of trace-backs that end at `lib.features.func_call_ability` which gives `ValueError: Unknown ability_id: 3674, type: cmd_quick. Likely a bug.`  Pastebin link with full bash CLI trace-back: [https://pastebin.com/FAVaQX7t](url).  

The issue seems to be with how the command is stored in the `actions.py` file, where stored in the `FUNCTIONS` variable, `FUNCTIONS` stores `Function.ability` objects in the notation `Function.ability(func_id, name, func, ab_id, general_id)`.  

However, `Attack_minimap` only has 4 items, not 5 like most of the rest of the items generated by `gen_actions.py`.  From `func_id` 12-20, they share `general_id` 3674.  Since `Attack_minimap` is missing `ab_id`, `general_id` somehow gets read in as `ab_id` in `lib.features.func_call_ability` and everything crashes, even though `lib.features.func_call_ability` does not throw this issue in the current master branch, with `FUNCTIONS` appearing unchanged from 7a04e74 to b07986f.  In the `FUNCTIONS` variable, changing `Attack_minimap.general_id` from 3674 to something like 13 or 14 (numbers specifically tested) causes the program not to crash any-more, but  `Attack_minimap` will not execute in any way, as the units will be selected, no action taken, then the step continued.

Picard MarkDuplicates denies the existence of the SAM 0x800 flag
issue_body:
I give up: https://sourceforge.net/p/samtools/mailman/message/32800679/. Supposedly this was fixed in Picard 1.96? That would make sense, if we weren't running:

```
$ docker run quay.io/ucsc_cgl/picardtools:1.95--dd5ac549b95eb3e5d166a5e310417ef13651994e --version
picard-tools
Copyright 2010 The Broad Institute
```

?????????
\<pained facial expression\>
More sleuthing:

```
$  apt-get changelog picard-tools
picard-tools (1.105-1) unstable; urgency=low

  * New upstream release 1.105
  * Conforms with Policy 3.9.5.

 -- Olivier Sallou <osallou@debian.org>  Tue, 24 Dec 2013 11:16:18 +0100
```

Which checks out when cross referenced with the Picard releases page.

[Anyways, as far as I can tell, the fix isn't to—you know—fix Picard](https://www.biostars.org/p/75693/), but rather, to run `bwa mem` with the `-M` flag ("Mark shorter split hits as secondary (for Picard compatibility).") because the correct way to fix a trivial bug is to introduce intentionally incorrect behavior into another tool.

[\<another pained expression\>](https://www.biostars.org/p/176814/)

Anyways, it's not clear if we can pass the `-M` flag via bwakit. I'm not much of a Perl person, but AFAIK, there's no way to [add to the bwa mem options here](https://github.com/lh3/bwa/blob/master/bwakit/run-bwamem#L146).

So, TBD.

issue_title: Finish EntityCreateMixin.create
issue_body:
Method [`EntityCreateMixin.create`](http://nailgun.readthedocs.org/en/latest/api/nailgun.html#nailgun.entity_mixins.EntityCreateMixin.create) is currently a thin wrapper around [`EntityCreateMixin.create_json`](http://nailgun.readthedocs.org/en/latest/api/nailgun.html#nailgun.entity_mixins.EntityCreateMixin.create_json). This is the case due to backwards compatibility reasons, but it needs to be changed so that NailGun can move forward. `EntitiyCreateMixin.create` should act similarly to [`EntityReadMixin.read`](http://nailgun.readthedocs.org/en/latest/api/nailgun.html#nailgun.entity_mixins.EntityReadMixin.read). It should call `EntityCreateMixin.create_json`, create a new entity of the current type, the new entity's fields, and return the new entity.

For more thorough background info on `EntityCreateMixin`, see [this blog post](http://www.ichimonji10.name/blog/4/).

Created from SatelliteQE/robottelo#1642

issue_title: Add python deps via pip install
issue_body:
When you `pip install nailgun` you don't get `requests` or `fauxfactory` pulled automatically, which causes issues if you try to use it. Should we add the following line to `setup.py`?

```python
install_requires=['fauxfactory', 'requests']
```

Alpine Linux Docker error when running .NET 2.1 - Error relocating /app/app-name/libcoreclr.so: gCurrentThreadInfo: symbol not found
issue_body:
# Issue Title

Alpine Linux Docker error when running .NET Core 2.1 - Error relocating /app/app-name/libcoreclr.so: gCurrentThreadInfo: symbol not found

# General

I created a Dockerfile to build and run a .NET Core 2.1 project using the new Alpine Linux images. When trying to run I get the following error: 

```
Failed to load X?j?U, error: Error relocating /app/reactive-dotnet/libcoreclr.so: gCurrentThreadInfo: symbol not found
Failed to bind to CoreCLR at '/app/reactive-dotnet/libcoreclr.so'
```

Below is my csproj file

```
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>netcoreapp2.1</TargetFramework>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.App" />
    <PackageReference Include="System.Reactive" Version="3.1.1" />
    <PackageReference Include="Npgsql" Version="3.2.7" />
    <PackageReference Include="Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL" Version="2.0.2" />
    <PackageReference Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="11.0.2" />
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <Folder Include="Model\" />
    <Folder Include="Controllers\" />
    <Folder Include="k8s\" />
    <Folder Include="k8s\postgres\" />
    <Folder Include="k8s\service\" />
    <Folder Include="commands\" />
    <Folder Include="database\" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

Below is the Dockerfile I'm using to build and run the app:

```
FROM microsoft/dotnet:2.1-sdk-alpine AS build
WORKDIR /app/app-name

COPY *.csproj .
RUN dotnet restore

COPY . .
RUN dotnet publish -c Release -r linux-x64 -o out

# This builds the actual container that would run in k8s
FROM microsoft/dotnet:2.1-runtime-alpine AS runtime

RUN apk update && \
    apk upgrade --available && \
    apk add --update --no-cache libc6-compat

WORKDIR /app/app-name
COPY --from=build /app/app-name/out ./

# Add a user so that the container does not run as root
RUN adduser -G root appuser -D && \
    chgrp -R 0 /app && \
    chmod -R g+rwx /app

# Run this as the appuser
USER appuser

EXPOSE 8080

CMD [ "dotnet", "app-name.dll" ]
```

I dug around a little bit and have been trying do install various packages to overcome this. So far, I have tried to add: 
* libcurl
* libgcc
* krb5-libs
* icu-libs
* lttng-ust-dev
* libssl1.0
* libstdc++
* libunwind
* libuuid
* zlib

Also, I tried removing the `USER appuser` line in the event that this was related to a permissions issue. 

After some digging, I'm assuming this has to do with needing a threading library of some sort. A few of the packages above having threading libraries as dependencies, but did not fix the issue. If I'm missing a package, no problem to install, just curious on how to get this running. Thanks!

issue_title: ResourceManager in .NET core does not fallback to unspecfic resource when using an unlocalized culture
issue_body:
Version: .NET Core App 1.1
OS Windows 7 (German)
Assembly System.Resources.ResourceManager, Version=4.0.0.0 
from %userprofile%\.nuget\packages\system.resources.resourcemanager\4.3.0\ref\netstandard1.0\System.Resources.ResourceManager.dll

I have a simple Resource named TestRes.resx plus three culture specific resources TestRes.de.resx, TestRes.en.resx and TestRes.it.resx.
There is one single key named "Test" with values indicating the used culture
Value from "de" resx
Value from "en" resx
Value from "it" resx
Value from unspecific resx.

Everything works just fine as long a I am using those cultures for which resx files do exist. But when calling GetString with another culture (i.e. "fr") the result (on my German OS) is: _Value from "de" resx_ instead of _Value from unspecific resx_

This behaviour is different from the .NET framework where using a culture for which no resx is given yields the value from the "default" resx (that without a culture suffix).

Am I missing something? Is this by design?

I've attached a zip file demonstrating the issue. There are two projects: a .NET Core Console App. The second one is a NET461 console app I added for comparison.

[LocalizationIssue.zip](https://github.com/dotnet/core/files/1128574/LocalizationIssue.zip)

https://github.com/django-tastypie/django-tastypie/blob/master/tastypie/resources.py#L2305
Is this a bug?
May be it should looks like this:
`setattr(related_obj, field_object.related_name, [bundle.obj])`

`setattr` calls `__set__` from `django.db.models.fields.related.ForeignRelatedObjectsDescriptor`
`__set__` calls `add(*value)` from `django.db.models.fields.related.RelatedManager`
where `value` must be a 'list' object

issue_title: obj_get used in resource_from_data can search through entire table when passed 'id':None
issue_body:
Hi,

Python version - 2.7.3 
Tastypie version - 0.13.3
Django version - 1.7.11

After upgrading from v12.1 -> v13.3 we encountered a problem in requests where we issued a PUT with a related object containing the data `{id:null}`.  These requests would try to query all the objects in the relevant table and ended up consuming all available memory.   

I believe I tracked it down to the change in #1363 from `obj_update` to `obj_get` in `resource_from_data` in fields.py.  `obj_get` generates its filters using `applicable_filters = self.build_filters(filters=kwargs, ignore_bad_filters=True)`.  `'id':None` seems to be considered a "bad filter" so it gets removed from applicable_filters which leads to calling `return self.get_object_list(request).filter(**applicable_filters)` without any filters, querying the entire table.

I'm not sure what the best solution is (or whether this is intended behavior) but the first two that come to mind are 
1.  Making `'id':None` a valid filter 
2.  Checking that applicable_filters is never empty (not sure if it's used elsewhere without any filters).


Thanks,
Patrick

issue_title: DKIM issues (py3)
issue_body:
I've noticed several issues with the manual DKIM signing:

1. pydkim seems to be abandoned, and is forked into dkimpy (https://launchpad.net/dkimpy)
2. dkimpy has issues with py3, e.g. `include_headers` has to be a list of byte strings on py3 (https://bugs.launchpad.net/dkimpy/+bug/1776775)

Is it worth having it after all, given that there is https://docs.aws.amazon.com/ses/latest/DeveloperGuide/easy-dkim.html (which is also mentioned in the docs by now)?

Maybe keep it, but document its problems?
As for include_headers expected to be bytes, that might get fixed eventually (or django-ses could cast it), but that's where I started wondering if it should be maintained further after all.

issue_title: why is str() the same as repr()?
issue_body:
```
>>> url = URL.from_text('https://example.org/api/v2')

>>> str(url)
"URL.from_text('https://example.org/api/v2')"

>>> repr(url)
"URL.from_text('https://example.org/api/v2')"
```

why is `str()` not just the url?

```
https://example.org/api/v2
```

the `requests` library "stringifies" urls (by design). in practice that means that for instance [furl](https://github.com/gruns/furl/) instances can be passed directly. this is not possible with `hyperlink`:

``` python
>>> from hyperlink import URL
>>> import requests

>>> url = URL.from_text('https://example.org/api/v2')
>>> requests.get(url)
[...]
InvalidSchema: No connection adapters were found for 'URL.from_text('https://example.org/api/v2')'
```

issue_title: LHCb uses dirac-proxy-init rather than lhcb-proxy-init
issue_body:
From what I can see, LHCb users call `dirac-proxy-init` as the LHCb plugin requires the `DiracProxy` credential to run stuff which has `dirac-proxy-init` hardcoded. Just talked to the Grid guys and this is a bad idea as in some cases, this puts RFC proxies in that are no longer supported. They suggest that the best way is to **always** call `lhcb-proxy-init` and the best way to do this that I can see is to create another Credential type (`LHCbProxy`) that calls this. 

What do people think?

P.S. This isn't massively urgent but needs to be fixed in the next couple of weeks!

But when I try to access 'script' field in response.data, Python raise KeyError. I checked it in debug mode and there is no field 'script' in response.data. Spider:
```
import scrapy
from scrapy_splash import SplashRequest


class SimpleSpider(scrapy.Spider):
    name = "simple"

    def start_requests(self):
        urls = [
            'https://www.fonbet.ru/#!/live',
        ]
        for url in urls:
            yield SplashRequest(url, self.parse,
                                args={

    def save_file(self, filename, content, write_flag='w+'):

            self.log('Saved file {}'.format(filename))

    def save_binary(self, filename, content):
        self.save_file(filename, content, 'w+b')

    def save_html(self, filename, content):

    def parse(self, response):

| Operating System & Version | Ubuntu 16.04  |
| Kernel Version (Linux Only)  | 4.4.0-98-generic  |
| SDK Version | 2.0 (master branch, Jan 24) |

I'm having an issue building the SDK from source with the Python bindings. I had managed to successfully do it under kernel version 4.13.0-31-generic but due to other reasons (CUDA 9.1 installation) I ended up downgrading to 4.4.0-98-generic.

I'm following the instructions in https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/blob/master/doc/installation.md with a couple of modifications:

  - Did not perform ```sudo apt-get dist-upgrade``` or ```sudo update-grub && sudo reboot``` since the newest 4.4.0-xxx kernel seems to have issues with the newest nvidia driver (could be wrong about that though)
  - inside the build directory ran ```cmake ../ -DBUILD_EXAMPLES=true -DBUILD_PYTHON_BINDINGS=bool:true```
  - ran ```make -j8```
  - Changed line 23 of patch-realsense-ubuntu-xenial.sh to ```sudo apt-get install linux-headers-$(uname -r) build-essential git``` since I'd want to install the headers for my kernel

The error shows up when running the patch-realsense-ubuntu-xenial.sh script. It runs almost to completion, but near the end gives the following error:
 ```
Handle uvcvideo :
Applying the patched module ... modprobe: ERROR: could not insert 'uvcvideo': Invalid argument

Failed to insert the patched module. Operation is aborted, the original module is restored

Verify that the current kernel version is aligned to the patched module version

modprobe: ERROR: could not insert 'uvcvideo': Invalid argument
```

If I comment out line 119 (try_module_insert uvcvideo ...), the script manages to finish, but clearly uvcvideo either wasn't restarted or wasn't patched. Trying to run realsense-viewer results in the application not finding any cameras connected.

I used synaptic to download the linux-image for 4.4.0-98, and it's headers. I did manage to forget to include linux-image-extras at one point, which resulted in other errors, so I would not rule out other kernel packages I could have missed.

Any ideas?

issue_title: LIBUVC backend and Windows 7
issue_body:
| Required Info  |   |
|---|---|
| Camera Model | R200 | 
| Firmware Version | 1.0.72.10  | 
| Operating System & Version | Windows 7  |
| Build System |  MinGW Makefile | 
Hello,
I have a following idea:
RealSense is not officially supported in Window 7 because of old Media Foundation driver. Maybe use libuvc instead? But doesn't UVC needs isochronous transfer ... which is again unsupported by WinUsb in Windows 7? That is true, but if ... 
1. Install libusbK driver for realsense (see Zadig http://zadig.akeo.ie/)
2. Build libusbx that supports isochronous transfer and works with libusbk (https://github.com/JoshBlake/libusbx). Steps 1&2 have been used to solve isotranfer for Kinect ( https://github.com/OpenKinect/libfreenect ) on windows and it worked! 
3. Build librealsense with RS_USE_LIBUVC_BACKEND instead of RS_USE_WMF_BACKEND. 

I have build librealsense using steps 1->2->3 with success. It works ... to a certain point.
- cpp-enumerate-devices example gives all interfaces and modes.
- examples (cpp-tutorial-1-depth or cpp-tutorial-2-streams) device has been found, UVC negotiations for given format is successfull (depth, camera, infrared2). There are two problems:
1. Interfaces are recognized correctly 0-infrared, 2-depth, 4-camera. But then for libusb_claim_interface it is asking for 1-3-5 (interface+1...and the do not exist :( ). I made some changes in realsense/src/libuvc code to overcome this. Then all interfaces are used correctly.
2. Unfortunatelly, even with previous changes, everything hangs in LIBUSB_TRANSFER_STALL. Any ideas? Any suggestions? 
I think this is important for many Windows 7 users which dont want to change to Win10 only for RealSense :)

... and .. please do not answear:
1. "it is impossible for windows 7" - everything is possible (see Kinect case :) )
2. "do not use MinGW, only VisualStudio" - your mother is using VisualStudio, I dont have to :)
3. "i have the same problem, without success..." - I know.

issue_body:
Hello, 

I'm trying to run an example code from the repository and it returns:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):

issue_title: ConnectionType(relay.Node) incorrectly prohibits connections to Node.
A limitation of Mutation?
request not being passed to filterclass
issue_body:
given the following code:
```
class ManageQuery(AbstractType):
    group_requests = DjangoFilterConnectionField(RequestNode, filterset_class=RequestManageFilter)
```
and the filterset class:
```
class RequestManageFilter(FilterSet):
    # Do case-insensitive lookups on 'name'

    @property
    def qs(self):
        user = getattr(self.request, 'user', None)
        print('User: ', user)
        parent = super(RequestManageFilter, self).qs
        return parent # .filter(group=self.request.user.main_author)

    class Meta:
        model = Request
        exclude = set()
```

the instance property self.request is always None, because when it's instantiated, graphene-django does not pass the context as request, shown here in fields.py's connection_resolver

```
   @classmethod
    def connection_resolver(cls, resolver, connection, default_manager, max_limit,
                            enforce_first_or_last, filterset_class, filtering_args,
                            root, args, context, info):
        filter_kwargs = {k: v for k, v in args.items() if k in filtering_args}

        qs = filterset_class(
            data=filter_kwargs,
            queryset=default_manager.get_queryset(),
        ).qs
```

passing request=context would make it work:
```
        qs = filterset_class(
            data=filter_kwargs,
            queryset=default_manager.get_queryset(),
            request=context,
        ).qs
```

issue_title: otThreadSetMasterkey
issue_body:
The api call is given by:
```
ThreadError otThreadSetMasterKey(otInstance *aInstance, const uint8_t *aKey, uint8_t aKeyLength)
```
If this is called with a masterkey that is not 16 bytes in length, e.g.
```
uint8_t key = {0x5E, 0xA1, 0xED};
otThreadSetMasterKey(aInstance, const &key, 3);
```
The 16 byte masterkey buffer will look like
```
0x5E, 0xA1, 0xED, 0x33, 0x44, 0x55, 0x66, 0x77, 0x88, 0x99, 0xAA, 0xBB, 0xCC, 0xDD, 0xEE, 0xFF
```
because of the default masterkey that was used before. (One could consider clearing the buffer first)

The above results in the following
1. The actual masterkey used is: `0x5E, 0xA1, 0xED, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00`
2. Getting the masterkey results in `0x5E, 0xA1, 0xED, 0x33, 0x44, 0x55, 0x66, 0x77, 0x88, 0x99, 0xAA, 0xBB, 0xCC, 0xDD, 0xEE, 0xFF` with length 16

If another thread devices joins, it will get the entire 16 byte buffer as masterkey. Since Thread does not provide a mechanic to communicate the masterkey's length to the new device, the joiner router and the joiner will have a different master key.

There are two issues:
1. The length field does not seem to be taken into account consistently (code problem)
2. The length field cannot be communicated to a new joiner device (api flaw)

issue_title: Wrong Xavier initialization for normally distributed weights.
issue_body: According to the documentation, when using the Xavier initialization with `uniform=False`, the weights are given a standard deviation of `sqrt(3. / (in + out))`. issue_title: Problems wrapping member function defined in base class
unchecked_mutable_reference element access methods should be const
issue_body:
## Issue description

The methods provided by `unchecked_mutable_reference` are not marked as `const`:
https://github.com/pybind/pybind11/blob/5ef1af138dc3ef94c05274ae554e70d64cd589bf/include/pybind11/numpy.h#L389
https://github.com/pybind/pybind11/blob/5ef1af138dc3ef94c05274ae554e70d64cd589bf/include/pybind11/numpy.h#L400
https://github.com/pybind/pybind11/blob/5ef1af138dc3ef94c05274ae554e70d64cd589bf/include/pybind11/numpy.h#L403
As `const`-ness is not transitive in C++, these three should all be marked as `const` (while they allow modifying the returned data, they themselved do not mutable the `unchecked_mutable_reference` object).

## Reproducible example code

```
#include <iostream>
#include <pybind11/numpy.h>

void foo(pybind11::detail::unchecked_mutable_reference<float, 1> const& x)
{
    // Assuming x has at least one element for brevity
    std::cout << x[0] << std::endl;
}
```

will fail to compile (even though it should) because `x` been passed as a constant reference (which is a common use case to avoid copies).

## Corrective action

Change the methods quoted above to:

`template <typename... Ix> T& operator()(Ix... index) const {`

`T &operator[](ssize_t index) const { return operator()(index); } `

`template <typename... Ix> T *mutable_data(Ix... ix) const { return &operator()(ssize_t(ix)...); }`

issue_title: Check for basic consistency.
issue_body:
From http://blog.zulip.org/2016/10/13/static-types-in-python-oh-mypy/:

Add the  --check-untyped-defs option to your mypy arguments, and get that running with no errors on the codebase. This option causes mypy to check every def in the codebase for internal consistency; mypy can detect many classes of bugs and mistakes in your codebase, without your having written a single type annotation!

In most cases, you’ll want to fix bugs and bad code as you go, but you can also use  #type: ignore annotations or exclude files to defer issues. For example, we excluded all of Zulip’s tests at first, since they’re both lower value to type-check and had most of the monkey-patching and other questionable Python in the project. For Zulip, getting clean  --check-untyped-defs output took me about 2 days of hard work, including merging fixes for about 40 issues in the Zulip codebase.

I spent another day or two generating nice reproducers for the mypy bugs I’d encountered and improving typeshed. Now that mypy is no longer in its infancy, mypy bugs are increasingly rare. But a large project should expect to encounter and fix typeshed issues (just submit a PR!).

issue_labels: [{'id': 277080619, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20code%20health', 'name': 'area: code health', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 263357278, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20testing', 'name': 'area: testing', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 149596829, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/good%20first%20issue', 'name': 'good first issue', 'color': 'c61aff', 'default': True}]
issue_body:
This issue is for checking untyped definitions in the `certbot-nginx` module.

See explanation here: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1fp0tPP9bzYkUViTnOXAyoT_KfgTfs8HSpNMoU8Tm_SY/edit?usp=sharing

From http://blog.zulip.org/2016/10/13/static-types-in-python-oh-mypy/:

Add the `--check-untyped-defs` option to your mypy arguments, and get that running with no errors on the codebase. This option causes mypy to check every def in the codebase for internal consistency; mypy can detect many classes of bugs and mistakes in your codebase, without your having written a single type annotation!

In most cases, you’ll want to fix bugs and bad code as you go, but you can also use #type: ignore annotations or exclude files to defer issues. For example, we excluded all of Zulip’s tests at first, since they’re both lower value to type-check and had most of the monkey-patching and other questionable Python in the project. For Zulip, getting clean `--check-untyped-defs` output took me about 2 days of hard work, including merging fixes for about 40 issues in the Zulip codebase.

I spent another day or two generating nice reproducers for the mypy bugs I’d encountered and improving typeshed. Now that mypy is no longer in its infancy, mypy bugs are increasingly rare. But a large project should expect to encounter and fix typeshed issues (just submit a PR!).

Once `mypy --check-untyped-defs` passes on your codebase, you should lock down your progress by running the mypy checker in your CI environment.

Because mypy type annotations are optional, once you’ve done the setup above, you can start annotating your codebase at whatever pace you like. Over time, you’ll get the benefits of static types in the parts of the codebase that you’ve annotated, without needing to do anything special to the rest of the codebase (the wonders of gradual typing!). In the next section, I’ll discuss strategy for how to move the codebase towards being fully annotated.

issue_title: [Question] Plugins without weird namespacing
issue_body:
Checked out master w/ venv to try see how plugins are developed.

Following the [instructions here](https://certbot.eff.org/docs/contributing.html#writing-your-own-plugin) for installing the example plugin, `certbot plugins` results in:

```
* certbot-example-plugins:example_authenticator
Description: Example Authenticator plugin
Interfaces: IAuthenticator, IPlugin
Entry point: example_authenticator = certbot_example_plugins:Authenticator

* certbot-example-plugins:example_installer
Description: Example Installer plugin
Interfaces: IInstaller, IPlugin
Entry point: example_installer = certbot_example_plugins:Installer
```

which means you have to use `-a certbot-example-plugins:example_installer`, `--certbot-example-plugins:example_installer-confoption blah` etc.

This effect also happens against my plugin which I stick in a directory at the root of the repo.

I can't figure out why this is happening or how to tune `setup.py` to stop the prefixing from happening like with the plugins that ship with the repo.

Any hints?

Thanks!

issue_title: Rebooting RPi3 with clf connected to the PN532 on UART locks the serial port
issue_body: While clf is open, rebooting or shutting down a raspberry pi 3 will cause the serial port (e.g. ttyAMA0) to be in a deadlock as the clf was not safely closed. This also happens when a sigkill is issued on a process using clf. Currently the only way to free up the port is to reconnect the PN532 but a reboot doesn't cut the power to the PN532.

issue_title: Option --create-groups should only factorize known attributes
issue_body:
I had a SVG with SMIL animation which I tried to optimize with scour (actually Inkscape but it turns out they use scour in backend. Ref: https://bugs.launchpad.net/inkscape/+bug/1638040).

Many <animate*> tags had a `fill` attribute with `freeze` value and scour therefore factorized them inside a single <g fill="freeze"> group. The problem is that the SVG spec says nowhere that the value of `fill` on the parent node is used when it is absent. It only says that `remove` is the default value (cf. https://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/single-page.html#animate-TimingAttributes). As a consequence the SVG animation ended broken since I was relying on pieces of animation getting frozen to their last frame.

The `create-groups` option should only create groups on attributes known to work with the parent's value.

issue_title: Action level signature
issue_body: Right now sos records signatures at step and task levels. When we work on a step with long running scripts, it might be helpful to have action level signature so that long-running scripts can be more easily mixed with other actions. in my case, adding another `run` action after a `run` action running `STAR` causes the whole `STAR` command to be re-executed.

issue_title: Batch size argument in ImageClassifierData.from_csv should not be default
issue_body:
The Batch size is assigned to a variable called `bs` but is not used for the construction of the DataLoader. I noticed this in **pascal.ipynb** and **pascal-multi.ipynb** but I suspect it could be in more places.
It can be fixed for example in **pascal.ipynb** like:
```python
md = ImageClassifierData.from_csv(PATH, JPEGS, CSV, tfms=tfms) # before
md = ImageClassifierData.from_csv(PATH, JPEGS, CSV, tfms=tfms, bs=bs) # after
```

issue_title: interactive and boxes don't play nicely on ipywidgets 7 and mpl inline backend
issue_body:
this piece of code doesn't work on jupyter (inline backend, as usual); not sure whether it's a bug or something that has changed recently in ipywidgets.

```python
import pandas as pd
from ipywidgets import RadioButtons as RB, HBox as HB
from IPython.display import display

def f(c):
    pd.DataFrame([1,2,3]).plot(title=c)

iw = interactive(f, **{"c": RB(options=["a", "b"])})
b = iw.children[:-1]
iw.children = [HB(b)]
display(iw)
```

naturally, this does

```python
import pandas as pd
from ipywidgets import RadioButtons as RB

def f(c):
    pd.DataFrame([1,2,3]).plot(title=c)

interactive(f, **{"c": RB(options=["a", "b"])})
```

issue_title: Output files from apps
issue_body:
If we have an app that produces no output files we call such an app like : 

    <var:App_fu> = app_func ( .... )

However if the app has outputs, the return is a tuple : 

    <var:App_fu>, [ <var:Data_fu> ... ] = app_func ( ...., outputs=[ Files ....])

When this was last discussed, one proposed solution was to simply return the app future in both cases, and have the data futures be a property of the app future. This would look like this : 

    # Single return
    app_fu = app_func ( ... , outputs=['a.txt', 'b.txt'] )
    # User has to unpack outputs separately 
    x, y = app_fu.outputs

Right now, we have the outputs property added, but continue to return a tuple of app future and the list of data futures. I propose that these changes should go into 0.3.0.

issue_title: RFC - Bash App definition change
issue_body:
We currently use a magic variable "cmd_line" in bash apps to specify the command-line string to be executed. For eg : 
``` python
@App('python', dfk)
def hello ( ):
   cmd_line = "echo 'Hello world' "
```
This is bad for a few reasons:
1. This is surprising behavior in python
2. There's ambiguity in behavior if the user were to reassign to cmd_line
3. Any values returned are certainly lost (again unusual behavior for python)
4. Implementation wise, we trace the value assigned to the magic variable and this is computationally expensive.

I recommend that we switch to having bash apps return a string, and that string be treated as the command line executable and if we decide that this change is acceptable, we should merge for 0.3.0. 
For eg:

``` python
@App('python', dfk)
def hello ( ):
   return "echo 'Hello World' "
```

issue_title: [QUESTION] Is it possible to collect multiple users at the same time?
issue_body:
Is it possible to collect from multiple users in parallel? I've tried

```
import twint
from multiprocessing import Process


def process(user):
    c = twint.Config()
    c.Username = user
    c.Since = '2018-07-01'
    c.Store_json = True
    c.Output = '{}.json'.format(user)
    twint.run.Search(c)


users = ['otojya', 'otsuichich']

for user in users:
    p = Process(target=process, args=(user,))
    p.start()
    p.join()
```

But it doesn't seem to run in parallel. It finishes collecting `otojya` and then it collects from `otsuichich`.
